highlights.title: Nvidia strikes AI cloud, memory deals in Korea - Light Reading
highlights.titleZh: Nvidia strikes AI cloud, memory deals in Korea - Light Reading
highlights.content: * [AI & Machine Learning](/digital-transformation/ai-machine-learning) * [Cloud](/digital-transformation/cloud) * [Data Centers](/it-infrastructure/data-centers) * [Network Automation](/digital-transformation/network-automation) * [News](/latest-news) # Nvidia strikes AI cloud, memory deals in Korea Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang clinches AI deals with SK Group, Naver and LG Group in Seoul, following a series of partnerships announced in Taipei last week. [Robert Clark,](/author/robert-clark)Contributing Editor,Light Reading June 9, 2026 2 Min Read (Source: Nvidia) Another week, another slew of Nvidia AI deals with Asian partners. Last week Nvidia boss Jensen Huang unveiled a series of partnerships on a visit to Taipei, including[with SKT on physical AI.](/ai-machine-learning/nvidia-taps-skt-for-physical-ai) Now he's in Seoul announcing multiple deals with Korean firms, notably with the SK Group, the parent of SK Telecom and long-time Nvidia supplier SK Hynix. The scale and breadth of these tie-ups underscore the importance of Korea not only to Nvidia's global supply chain but also as a place that can validate its tech at scale.  In a joint briefing with Huang at SK headquarters on Monday, SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won said the new agreements signaled a deeper alliance that goes beyond just memory chips, Korea Herald[reported](https://www.koreaherald.com/article/10766123). "Most of our cooperation so far has been centered on memory, but from now on, we will raise the partnership to the SK Group level," Chey said. In the most significant announcement,[SK Hynix and Nvidia struck](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/sk-hynix-ai-factory) a multi-year deal for memory supply and joint R&D on next-generation memory and AI architecture. Related:[Nvidia taps SKT for physical AI](/ai-machine-learning/nvidia-taps-skt-for-physical-ai) ##### AI cloud Nvidia said the partnership would enable memory supply "to keep pace with Nvidia's infrastructure roadmap and the sustained buildout of AI infrastructure worldwide." "Together, we are codeveloping the next generation of memory for AI factories and applying AI to how we design and manufacture semiconductors," Chey said. SK Telecom's role in the enlarged partnership is to [build a gigawatt AI cloud](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/sk-telecom-ai-infrastructure), expected to come online in 2027, Nvidia said.  The GPU-based cloud is aimed at serving rising demand for AI infrastructure and will be built on Nvidia's[DSX platform](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/products/dsx/), designed to produce low-cost tokens at high energy efficiency. It will support training, inference and agentic workloads, including sovereign AI, initially for South Korea but with plans to extend to elsewhere in Asia.  Nvidia also announced AI infrastructure deals with Naver and the LG Group. Naver, whose services range from search to shopping and cloud,[will use Nvidia chips to grow its sovereign AI infrastructure](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/naver-ai-infrastructure), starting at 55MW but with the aim of expanding to gigawatt scale. It will also use Nvidia chips to accelerate its data center business and to boost its sovereign AI capacity in Europe and the Middle East. Nvidia's[partnership with LG](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-and-lg-group-ai-factory/) covers multiple emerging technologies including robotics, autonomous driving, data centers and cloud. The AI factory will provide LG Group with accelerated computing infrastructure to train and validate AI-based applications across the company, Nvidia said. ## About the Author [Robert Clark](/author/robert-clark) Contributing Editor, Light Reading Robert Clark is an independent technology editor and researcher based in Hong Kong. [See more from Robert Clark](/author/robert-clark) ## Want more Light Reading stories in your Google search results? highlights.content: # LG Teams with NVIDIA to Shape the Future with M.A.P. (Mobility / AI Infra / Physical AI) 2026-06-08     PR Newswire SEOUL, South Korea, June 8, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- LG and NVIDIA are expanding their strategic collaboration across industries, Physical AI, AI Infra and mobility jointly drawing the future map of industry. LG and NVIDIA held a Top Management Meeting in Yeouido, Seoul, attended by Kwang Mo Koo, Chairman and CEO of LG Corp., Jensen Huang, CEO of NVIDIA, and other top executives. The meetings, attended by Kwang Mo Koo, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, and heads of major companies, expand the scope of mid- to long-term strategic cooperation leading industrial innovation in the AI era. The expanded collaboration combines NVIDIA's cutting-edge AI technologies with LG's manufacturing and infrastructure capabilities to implement AI most quickly and comprehensively in customers' daily lives and industrial sites. The collaboration between LG, which possesses decades of manufacturing innovation know-how and vast life data assets accumulated through customer touchpoints around the world, and NVIDIA, is expected to accelerate global AI innovation across both industry and daily life. Kwang Mo Koo, Chairman and CEO of LG Corp., said, "Together with NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, we had a very in-depth and inspiring discussion on strategic cooperation that will transform future industries," adding, "The blueprint for the AI ecosystem envisioned by NVIDIA aligns with LG's future direction of creating meaningful changes in customers' daily lives and global industrial sites. With today's meeting as a starting point, we will further solidify our partnership by combining the unique capabilities of both companies." "Korea is extraordinary at manufacturing, mechatronics and AI, and the fusion of these strengths will make robotics and physical AI a major growth sector for the country," said Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of NVIDIA. "With NVIDIA DSX and physical AI platforms, LG can extend its leadership from homes and vehicles to factories and AI infrastructure, creating new growth opportunities across the intelligent systems that will shape daily life and industry." **1. Cooperation in Physical AI and Robotics** The two companies will pursue a win-win strategy in the field of Physical AI, spanning manufacturing to robotics, by maximizing synergies based on each company's core capabilities. LG has accumulated production technology data and know-how from global manufacturing sites, while NVIDIA provides NVIDIA Isaac, Omniverse and Cosmos AI and simulation technologies. By combining these strengths, the two companies plan to enhance AI-driven manufacturing competitiveness, build an autonomous manufacturing ecosystem in which the entire process from raw material procurement to production, logistics, and customer delivery is connected in real time through data and AI, and establish it as a new global smart factory standard. The two companies will also pursue development of NVIDIA's next-generation robot foundation model GR00T, further strengthening cooperation in robotics. LG and NVIDIA will advance robot development capabilities and performance through strategic cooperation across the entire robotics field, from data collection and generation, simulation, training, and actions, in a wide range of robots including humanoids and logistics robots. LG Innotek will serve as the eyes and ears of robots based on its world-class optical technologies, and will develop high-performance sensing modules and optical components optimized for NVIDIA AI infrastructure. Furthermore, LG CNS is building an ecosystem that enables anyone to easily adopt AI robots in manufacturing and logistics sites. By integrating NVIDIA's robotics technologies, including NVIDIAIsaac open robotics frameworks, Cosmos open world models and Isaac GR00T open robotic foundation models, into its industrial robot platform, "PhysicalWorks," the company is accelerating the AI transformation of logi...

highlights.content: * [About us](https://www.exchangewire.com/about-us/) * [Contact Us](https://www.exchangewire.com/contact/) * [Homepage](https://www.exchangewire.com/ "Homepage") * [Agentic AI](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/agentic-ai/ "Agentic AI") * ExchangeWire PressBox in [Agentic AI](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/agentic-ai/)[Media](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/media/)[Revenue](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/revenue/) 1 hour ago # ADvendio Launches Specialised AI Workforce to Drive Agentic Advertising **[ADvendio](https://www.advendio.com?utm_source=chatgpt.com), a leading revenue OS for agentic advertising announced today (8th June, 2026) the launch of a new category of agentic AI that marks the first time publishers, broadcasters, and retail media networks will have access to agents that are built specifically for the advertising industry.** To date, advertising teams have been reliant on AI chatbots with limited scope and understanding of this specific market. Now, the launch of the first Specialised Agentic Workforce signals a new era of autonomous co-workers capable of executing workflows and scaling operations with minimal manual intervention. ADvendio’s Specialised Agentic Workforce is different from other agents as it has been designed and built from the ground up for media operations environments. This is important in particular as it increases the value and usefulness of the tasks these agents can handle, allowing team leaders to delegate operations from advertising sales to campaign execution with higher confidence. Powered by AdCP, these digital co-workers can instantly co-ordinate with each other and existing enterprise systems to streamline the entire advertising lifecycle. "The advertising industry has lacked access to a unique agentic AI solution that can actually perform high-value tasks for too long," said Julian Ahrends, CTO at ADvendio. "With increasing pressure to grow revenue across omnichannel campaigns that grow more complex by the day, we’re proud that the Specialised Agentic Workforce from ADvendio is being introduced at a pivotal moment for advertising and media leaders, bringing advanced autonomous capabilities directly into our AdOne, AdFinance, AdGateway, and AdPortal solutions." The launch introduces an initial suite of nine specialised AI agent roles, strategically grouped across three core pillars of media operations—sales, ad ops, and finance—to eliminate systemic industry bottlenecks. To accelerate sales, the seller agent acts as an always-on, profit-optimised sales rep to scale client acquisition; the sales-enablement agent provides critical insights to close deals faster; the proposal agent instantly creates proposals to maintain pipeline momentum; and the campaign rebooking agent clones successful campaigns for repeat revenue. For ad ops, the targeting agent pinpoints ideal audience segments and inventory to hit campaign goals; the optimisation agent autonomously adjusts budgets and shifts spend across omnichannel environments to maximise campaign yield; and the inventory agent allows internal teams to check availability and get real-time delivery updates via a simple chat interface. Finally, the finance suite streamlines back-office administration: the invoicing agent executes precise billing runs instantly with a single command, while the month-end agent autonomously organises line items and cross-references delivery data against contract terms to drastically accelerate closing timelines. The result is significantly faster reconciliation cycles, reduced billing errors, and improved financial transparency across advertising operations. The operational benefits for media businesses are substantial. By automating traditionally manual processes enterprises can eliminate administrative backlogs that often delay campaign execution, reporting and billing. highlights.title: Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models - Anadolu Ajansı
highlights.titleZh: Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models - Anadolu Ajansı
highlights.content: Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models [World](/en/world) # Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models Anthropic reports 80% of Claude's code is self-generated and projects future models will continuously develop their own successors despite growing warnings Abdulkadir Günyol 09 June 2026•Update: 09 June 2026 Growing calls around the world to slow or halt the development of artificial intelligence (AI) are colliding with rapid advances that are bringing the technology closer to developing future versions of itself with limited human involvement. US technology firm Anthropic recently added to those concerns by publishing a report suggesting the threshold for autonomous AI development may be closer than previously believed. The company said more than 80% of the code powering its Claude chatbot is now generated by the model itself, compared with a share in the single digits in early 2025. According to Anthropic's AI development framework, current technology has reached the fourth of five stages, where models can autonomously execute code and delegate tasks to other systems. The fifth and final stage is expected to involve autonomous agents capable of designing and training their own models, potentially allowing future AI systems to continuously develop successor versions. The developments come despite repeated warnings from academics, technology leaders and public figures about the risks associated with increasingly advanced AI systems. One of the most prominent warnings came in March 2023, when the Future of Life Institute published an open letter signed by figures including Elon Musk, calling for a six-month pause on training AI systems more powerful than GPT-4. The letter warned that AI systems approaching human-level intelligence could pose significant risks, including massive job losses, widespread misinformation and a potential loss of human control over civilization. Pope Leo XIV also addressed the issue in his encyclical Magnifica Humanitas, released in May, calling for a halt to AI development and urging countries to separate the technology from military and economic competition. The scale of global investments into the sector is one of its primary obstacles in slowing the rapid development despite these global pleas for caution. Global spending on AI is projected to rise 47% year-on-year in 2026 to $2.59 trillion, before increasing to $3.49 trillion in 2027, according to a Gartner report released in May. \*Writing by Emir Yildirim in Istanbul news\_share news\_share\_description**[subscription\_contact](https://www.aa.com.tr/tr/p/abonelik-talep-formu/1001)** [subscription](https://www.aa.com.tr/tr/p/abonelik-talep-formu/1001) Previous Previous [### Amnesty warns US travel curbs could prevent thousands of fans from attending 2026 World Cup](/en/sports/amnesty-warns-us-travel-curbs-could-prevent-thousands-of-fans-from-attending-2026-world-cup/3960828) [## LATEST NEWS](/en/latest) ### [Trump says US will declare 'total victory' over Iran within 2 weeks](/en/us-israel-iran-war/trump-says-us-will-declare-total-victory-over-iran-within-2-weeks/3960871) ### [Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models](/en/world/global-calls-to-halt-ai-development-clash-with-prospect-of-self-improving-models/3960870) ### [Pentagon adds Alibaba, Baidu, BYD to list of firms allegedly aiding Chinese military](/en/americas/pentagon-adds-alibaba-baidu-byd-to-list-of-firms-allegedly-aiding-chinese-military/3960869) ### [Bolivia’s president signs law giving him new emergency powers as siege enters second month](/en/americas/bolivia-s-president-signs-law-giving-him-new-emergency-powers-as-siege-enters-second-month/3960867) ### [US Senator Sanders vows to block defense bill provision integrating American, Israeli defense sectors](/en/americas/us-senator-sanders-vows-to-block-defense-bill-provision-integrating-american-israeli-defense-sec...

Audit and advisory solutions provider Fieldguide announced the launch of Field Orchestrator, touted as a [long-horizon](https://jumpcloud.com/it-index/what-is-long-horizon-planning-in-ai) agent capable of completing substantive testing workflows. Processing Content While the specific [definition](https://www.ai21.com/glossary/ai-agent/what-are-long-horizon-tasks/) can [vary](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/what-long-horizon-agent-memory-why-ai-agents-forget-fix-kanis-patel-kjf5f/), in general a long-horizon agent can be described as an AI agent that is used not for singular tasks but managing complex goals over days, weeks or months, executing thousands of individual steps while maintaining a persistent state and memory the whole time to keep it focused on its function. Purpose-built for audit and advisory to plan and execute engagements from start to finish, Field Orchestrator is said to sustain reasoning and action across the full arc of an engagement, coordinating multi-agent workflows without continuous practitioner oversight. It treats testing not as a discrete task to be done in response to specific prompts but as a continuous workflow across population analysis, sample selection, evidence collection, extraction, testing, and workpaper generation. highlights.content: [Skip to main content](#main-content) # Nvidia's AI PC push banks on unproven demand beyond niche users By [Aditya Soni](/authors/aditya-soni/) and [Anhata Rooprai](/authors/anhata-rooprai/) * Companies * [Advanced Micro Devices Inc](/company/advanced-micro-devices-inc/) * [Apple Inc](/company/apple-inc/) * [Asustek Computer Inc](/company/asustek-computer-inc/) June 8 (Reuters) - Nvidia's [(NVDA.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/NVDA.O) entry into the AI PC market with its [RTX Spark superchip](https://www.reuters.com/world/china/nvidia-ceo-kick-off-dominate-computex-gathering-taipei-2026-05-31/) last week is less a breakthrough for regular users than a high-stakes bet that a largely unproven concept can find wider appeal, analysts said. At the Computex trade show in Taiwan, ​the chipmaker pitched a future where laptops run large AI models locally and act as personal digital agents, no cloud needed. The Reuters Inside Track newsletter is your essential guide during the World Cup. Sign up [here.](/newsletters/inside-track/?location=article-paragraph&redirectUrl=%2Fworld%2Fchina%2Fnvidias-ai-pc-push-banks-unproven-demand-beyond-niche-users-2026-06-08%2F) It's ‌a claim that PC makers HP [(HPQ.N), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/HPQ.N) and Dell [(DELL.N), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/DELL.N) have made for nearly three years now, only to be met with skepticism from Wall Street and consumers, with high prices outweighing tangible benefits. Nvidia, though, appears to be selling a different version of the AI PC than what exists today, one aimed more at developers and content creators who ​have long favored Apple's [(AAPL.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/AAPL.O) high-end MacBook Pros. Six companies - Microsoft [(MSFT.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/MSFT.O), Asus [(2357.TW), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/2357.TW), HP, Lenovo [(0992.HK), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/0992.HK), Dell, and MSI [(2377.TW), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/2377.TW) - will make PCs using the chip. ​These stocks surged after Nvidia's announcement on June 1. "RTX Spark doesn't make traditional PCs obsolete. It creates a ⁠new category between the workstation and the AI server," said Kevin Hein, analyst at Tirias Research. The chip combines a central processor, graphics engine and up ​to 128 gigabytes of unified memory, making it capable of running large AI models locally, something current AI PCs cannot do at scale. Nvidia says ​it could reshape how people interact with computers, with AI agents handling tasks such as generating videos or debugging code. Existing AI PCs, marketed heavily over the past two years, have centered on modest features like transcription or image editing and have failed to drive meaningful sales for the device manufacturers and their partners such as Arm ​and Qualcomm [(QCOM.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/QCOM.O). ## COST BARRIERS LOOM A premium price and a memory chip crunch, which has already driven up device cost, are likely to limit RTX Spark ​devices to niche adoption, analysts said. The cost "won't deter all the big computer makers from working with Nvidia on this, but the bulk of PC sales for the next ‌several years ⁠will still be more traditional Windows-based PCs with chips from Intel [(INTC.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/INTC.O), AMD and Qualcomm," said Bob O'Donnell, president at TECHnalysis Research. HP and Dell stock had been climbing even before the launch of Nvidia's superchip, up 18% and 223%, respectively, so far this year. But that rally has been driven less by AI PCs and more by a wave of corporate upgrades to Windows 11, as well as booming demand for AI infrastructure, particularly for Dell. highlights.titleZh: LG Uplus bets big on AI data centers with $3.26B target - Light Reading
highlights.content: * [Data Centers](/it-infrastructure/data-centers) * [AI & Machine Learning](/digital-transformation/ai-machine-learning) * [Business Management](/business-management) * [News](/latest-news) # LG Uplus bets big on AI data centers with $3.26B target LG Uplus has placed its upcoming 200 MW Paju AI Data Center at the heart of its AIDC infrastructure strategy as it pursues inference-driven AI workloads. [Gigi Onag,](/author/gigi-onag)Senior Editor, APAC,Light Reading June 8, 2026 3 Min Read (Source: Olga Salt/Alamy Stock Photo) South Korean telecom operator LG Uplus is ramping up its AI data center (AIDC) infrastructure business with a plan to achieve 5 trillion Korean won (US$3.26 billion) in cumulative orders by 2030, growing annual sales by 15% to 20%. At the heart of the company's AIDC infrastructure strategy is the 200MW hyperscale facility now being built in Paju, Gyeonggi Province.  The facility will consist of five buildings spanning approximately 150,000 square meters (the equivalent of 21 soccer fields). It is said to be the only data center in the Seoul metropolitan area capable of supporting such a high level of power. This positions the facility as a key hub for inference-driven AI workloads. In a [statement](https://www.lguplus.com/about/en/corporation/promotion/press-kit/detail?atclNo=2000001574&pageNo=1&srchCond=titCon&srchCntn=) released last Friday, LG Uplus noted that the shift in focus of AI operations from training to "inference" has led to increased power consumption, volatility and a sharp rise in heat generation in AI data centers. Additionally, constructing data centers takes years, creating a gap between the demand and supply of AI infrastructure. Related:[AtlasEdge lands financing for European expansion](/data-centers/atlasedge-lands-financing-for-european-expansion) "The GPUs needed for AI work can be secured within a few months, but building AIDCs to accommodate them takes three to four years, which widens the gap. Additionally, AI token usage has more than doubled every quarter, highlighting delays in building AI data centers as a key risk to business progress," the company explained. ##### Accelerated AIDC construction LG Uplus plans to adopt a prefabricated modular data center construction method to accelerate time to deployment. This approach enables the offsite manufacturing of standardized main components and their onsite assembly, reducing build time by several months. It also enables the company to scale proof-of-concept projects to hyperscale facilities flexibly. The company will also rely on the "One LG" alliance to build its Paju AIDC, leveraging specialized, localized technologies across the LG Group ecosystem. LG Electronics will develop and provide advanced thermal cooling units and free-cooling chillers. LG Energy Solutions will supply high-performance uninterruptible power supply (UPS) batteries with multi-layered safety cells, which will eliminate the risk of fire and thermal runaway. LG Uplus and LS Electric will jointly develop the massive DC 800V power distribution system to handle high-density power loads. At the heart of the company's AIDC infrastructure strategy is the 200MW hyperscale facility now being built in Paju, Gyeonggi Province.  The facility will consist of five buildings spanning approximately 150,000 square meters (the equivalent of 21 soccer fields). highlights.content: [Skip to main content](#main-content) [Axios](/) 6 mins ago - [Business](https://www.axios.com/business) # Axios C-Suite: 3 new AI developments for the week of June 6 * [Andrew Kay](https://www.axios.com/authors/andrew.kay@axios.com) [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) Add Axios as your preferred source to see more of our stories on Google. [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) **1. Fast action AI software:** OpenAI [launched Sites](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly9kZXZlbG9wZXJzLm9wZW5haS5jb20vY29kZXgvc2l0ZXM_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uZXdzbGV0dGVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPW5ld3NsZXR0ZXJfY3N1aXRlX2JldGEmc3RyZWFtPXRvcA/586d9e571e560373298b467cBc1d0f48f), a Codex feature that lets your staff create, host and deploy work-related web apps from prompts internally — instantly. * **Why it matters:** Custom software usually requires vendor contracts, engineering time or a long IT queue. Non-engineers can skip all that and build, ship and USE internal tools themselves. **2. AI's cheaper future:** Nvidia's RTX Spark and Microsoft's Project Solara [aim to move](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDUvMzAvbnZpZGlhLW1pY3Jvc29mdC1wY3MtYWktc3VyZmFjZS1kZWxsP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9bmV3c2xldHRlciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1uZXdzbGV0dGVyX2NzdWl0ZV9iZXRhJnN0cmVhbT10b3A/586d9e571e560373298b467cBe2976078) AI inference from the cloud onto personal devices. * **Why it matters:** Companies are starting to ration AI [as token costs rise](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDUvMTgvdG9rZW5zLWFpLXNvZnR3YXJlLWNvc3QtcHJlcC1jZW8_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uZXdzbGV0dGVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPW5ld3NsZXR0ZXJfY3N1aXRlX2JldGEmc3RyZWFtPXRvcA/586d9e571e560373298b467cBbf685d8d). Local [AI](https://www.axios.com/technology/automation-and-ai) will make usage less metered, like a shift from paying by the minute for long-distance calls to an unlimited plan. **3. Physical AI's GPT moment:** Nvidia [unveiled Cosmos 3](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDYvMDEvbnZpZGlhLWFpLXB1c2gtY29zbW9zLTMtd29ybGQtbW9kZWw_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uZXdzbGV0dGVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPW5ld3NsZXR0ZXJfY3N1aXRlX2JldGEmc3RyZWFtPXRvcA/586d9e571e560373298b467cB18c5392a), a foundation model for physical AI. * **Why it matters:** The first wave of AI read and wrote. The next has to see, move and react to power a factory robot reaching for a part. Trained on physics rather than text, Cosmos 3 aims to be the infrastructure for that shift. 📈 *If you're a CEO or on a CEO's team: [Ask to join](https://www.axios.com/newsletters/c-suite?utm_medium=native&utm_source=axios_article&utm_campaign=csuite) Jim's new weekly Axios C-Suite newsletter.* [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) ##### What to read next

highlights.content: [Latest News](/news) [## Home Affairs opens internal "conversation" on adopting three types of AI](/news/home-affairs-opens-internal-conversation-on-adopting-three-types-of-ai-626329) [## Superloop self-serve AI resolutions top 330,000 cases](/news/superloop-self-serve-ai-resolutions-top-330000-cases-626442) [## Meta accuses NSO Group of violating court order by WhatsApp spear phishing](/news/meta-accuses-nso-group-of-violating-court-order-by-whatsapp-spear-phishing-626482) [## Tarocash owner Retail Apparel Group to give HR systems an AI boost](/news/tarocash-owner-retail-apparel-group-to-give-hr-systems-an-ai-boost-626064) [## Meta accuses Australia of breaching free trade agreement](/news/meta-accuses-australia-of-breaching-free-trade-agreement-626439) * [Home](https://www.itnews.com.au) * [News](https://www.itnews.com.au/news) * [Business](https://www.itnews.com.au/business) * [Strategy](https://www.itnews.com.au/business/strategy) # Home Affairs opens internal "conversation" on adopting three types of AI By [Ry Crozier](/author/ry-crozier-149259) Jun 9 2026 6:35AM ## As CIO addresses broader prioritisation challenges. Home Affairs considers itself to be closer to the start of its adoption of AI, with the present focus on framing a department-wide “conversation” about the technology after earlier behind-the-scenes preparatory work. Speaking at the ServiceNow Federal Forum in Canberra, CIO and first assistant secretary of digital delivery services Matt Hay said Home Affairs had an interest in three forms of AI: assisted, embedded and agentic. “We’re trying to position conversations in the context of the notion of assisted AI, Copilot being an obvious example there,” Hay said. Embedded AI is more related to AI capabilities built into existing platforms, such as ServiceNow, which Home Affairs [uses for SecOps](https://www.itnews.com.au/news/home-affairs-adds-secops-to-new-cyber-risk-overhaul-619753) and [other security-oriented use cases.](https://www.itnews.com.au/news/home-affairs-digitises-authority-to-operate-process-for-ict-systems-612567) “ServiceNow is a really good example [of embedded AI]. We’re a ServiceNow user, it comes with AI capability. How do we want to unlock that?” Hay said. Lastly, the department is looking at agentic AI although it was not clear what the specific use cases for this might be. “We’re trying to frame conversations and a strategy and approach across those three [AI] domains,” Hay said. “We're very much, I think, right now at the start in the assisted space and what we can do from [a] productivity [perspective], and we've also organisationally looked at it from an operating model perspective,” he said. The department laid foundations for a more coordinated internal conversation on data and AI earlier this year when it restructured, bringing “data and AI capabilities in the organisation much closer into a technology group”. “Now we're thinking about how we operationalise that,” Hay said. “I think one of the things that we're really thinking about is how do we create some sort of incubator where we can look at really deliberate use cases, start slow and steady to prove value and then how do we then get them into broader use. “That's where we're at at the moment.” **Broader prioritisation focus** Hay said that more broadly in the technology space at Home Affairs, one of the main challenges is around “prioritisation” of activities and even resourcing applied to the various platforms and applications under its purview. “Home Affairs is a very diverse portfolio, with lots of challenges,” Hay said. “I think right now our biggest challenge is prioritisation. Demand, particularly for IT-enabled change, always exceeds our ability to resource that and fund it, so how do we prioritise effectively across different domains, which are really important to the country. “You've got trade and cargo, you've got national security, you've got securing our borders through travel movements. We've got to think about inv...

highlights.content: [Try Gloria Terminal](https://www.itsgloria.ai/) [Earn with Nexo](https://nexo.com/?utm_source=cryptobriefing&utm_campaign=cryptobriefing_button_earn_website) #### Sections [Bitcoin](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/bitcoin/) [DeFi](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/defi/) [Ethereum](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/ethereum/) [NFTs](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/nfts/) [Meme Coins](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/meme-coins/) [AI Agents](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/ai-agents/) [Regulation](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/regulation/) [Web3](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/web3/) [Business](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/business/) [Ecosystem](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/ecosystem/) [Earn with Nexo](https://nexo.com/?utm_source=cryptobriefing&utm_campaign=cryptobriefing_button_earn_website) # MIT researchers develop self-evolving AI scientists for scientific discovery A new category-theoretic framework lets AI systems rewrite their own reasoning rules, moving beyond pattern matching toward genuine scientific breakthroughs. Share [Add us on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=cryptobriefing.com "Add Crypto Briefing as your preferred source to see more of our stories on Google.") by [Editorial Team](https://cryptobriefing.com/team/editorial-team/) Most AI systems are smart the way a very fast librarian is smart. They find patterns in existing data, retrieve relevant information, and organize it neatly. What they don’t do is have an “aha” moment. They don’t realize their entire way of thinking about a problem is wrong, tear up the playbook, and start fresh with better concepts. That’s the gap MIT researchers Fiona Y. Wang and Markus J. Buehler are trying to close. Their new preprint, published May 31 on arXiv, lays out a formal mathematical framework that would allow AI systems to revise their own reasoning structures, not just optimize within the rules they were given. ## From search to genuine discovery The paper, titled “Self-Revising Discovery Systems for Science: A Categorical Framework for Agentic Artificial Intelligence,” draws a sharp line between three things that sound similar but are fundamentally different: retrieval, search, and discovery. Retrieval is looking something up. Search is exploring a known space for something new. Discovery, the hard one, means recognizing that the space itself needs to change. Advertisement The MIT framework uses a branch of mathematics called category theory to formalize this distinction. Specifically, it employs constructs known as copresheaves and provenance categories to represent how AI systems handle data and scientific claims. These aren’t just abstract decorations. They serve as the scaffolding that lets the system track where its knowledge comes from and, crucially, identify when that knowledge structure is no longer sufficient. The framework uses mathematical tools called left Kan extensions to ensure that when the AI transitions from one reasoning regime to another, the shift is formally validated. It’s not just guessing that a new approach might work. It’s proving, in a mathematical sense, that the new schema correctly extends what came before. ## Real problems, not toy demos Theoretical elegance is nice. But Wang and Buehler backed it up with two practical implementations that tackle real materials science problems. The first, called Builder/Breaker, addresses protein mechanics. Builder/Breaker uses the categorical framework to let the AI restructure its approach to these multi-scale challenges rather than just throwing more compute at a fixed model. The second implementation, CategoryScienceClaw, takes on fiber-network modeling. CategoryScienceClaw applies the self-revising framework to discover new ways of representing and reasoning about these structures. Both implementations treat data and scientific claims as what the paper calls “typed artifacts,” meaning every piece of information carries metadata about wh...

All eyes are on Apple as the company spent Monday trying to answer the question that's hung over the company since 2024: Can it finally deliver on the future it promised? At its [2026 Worldwide Developers Conference](https://www.businessinsider.com/apple-wwdc-ios-27-announcements-iphone-photos-parental-controls-2026-6) on Monday, the iPhone maker unveiled [Siri AI](https://www.businessinsider.com/apple-new-siri-ai-chatbot-app-wwdc-2026-6), a long-awaited overhaul of its voice assistant, powered by a new generation of Apple Intelligence models developed in [partnership with Google](https://www.businessinsider.com/apple-siri-bet-ai-models-commodities-google-gemini-2026-1). The company spent the past two years facing criticism and a [class action lawsuit](https://www.businessinsider.com/hey-siri-settlement-administrator-is-sending-letters-to-apple-users-2025-5) after many Apple Intelligence capabilities it previewed in 2024 were delayed or failed to arrive as initially promised. AI Agents.content: * [About us](https://www.exchangewire.com/about-us/) * [Contact Us](https://www.exchangewire.com/contact/) * [Homepage](https://www.exchangewire.com/ "Homepage") * [Agentic AI](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/agentic-ai/ "Agentic AI") * ExchangeWire PressBox in [Agentic AI](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/agentic-ai/)[Media](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/media/)[Revenue](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/revenue/) 1 hour ago # ADvendio Launches Specialised AI Workforce to Drive Agentic Advertising **[ADvendio](https://www.advendio.com?utm_source=chatgpt.com), a leading revenue OS for agentic advertising announced today (8th June, 2026) the launch of a new category of agentic AI that marks the first time publishers, broadcasters, and retail media networks will have access to agents that are built specifically for the advertising industry.** To date, advertising teams have been reliant on AI chatbots with limited scope and understanding of this specific market. Now, the launch of the first Specialised Agentic Workforce signals a new era of autonomous co-workers capable of executing workflows and scaling operations with minimal manual intervention. ADvendio’s Specialised Agentic Workforce is different from other agents as it has been designed and built from the ground up for media operations environments. This is important in particular as it increases the value and usefulness of the tasks these agents can handle, allowing team leaders to delegate operations from advertising sales to campaign execution with higher confidence. Powered by AdCP, these digital co-workers can instantly co-ordinate with each other and existing enterprise systems to streamline the entire advertising lifecycle. "The advertising industry has lacked access to a unique agentic AI solution that can actually perform high-value tasks for too long," said Julian Ahrends, CTO at ADvendio. "With increasing pressure to grow revenue across omnichannel campaigns that grow more complex by the day, we’re proud that the Specialised Agentic Workforce from ADvendio is being introduced at a pivotal moment for advertising and media leaders, bringing advanced autonomous capabilities directly into our AdOne, AdFinance, AdGateway, and AdPortal solutions." The launch introduces an initial suite of nine specialised AI agent roles, strategically grouped across three core pillars of media operations—sales, ad ops, and finance—to eliminate systemic industry bottlenecks. To accelerate sales, the seller agent acts as an always-on, profit-optimised sales rep to scale client acquisition; the sales-enablement agent provides critical insights to close deals faster; the proposal agent instantly creates proposals to maintain pipeline momentum; and the campaign rebooking agent clones successful campaigns for repeat revenue. For ad ops, the targeting agent pinpoints ideal audience segments and inventory to hit campaign goals; the optimisation agent autonomously adjusts budgets and shifts spend across omnichannel environments to maximise campaign yield; and the inventory agent allows internal teams to check availability and get real-time delivery updates via a simple chat interface. Finally, the finance suite streamlines back-office administration: the invoicing agent executes precise billing runs instantly with a single command, while the month-end agent autonomously organises line items and cross-references delivery data against contract terms to drastically accelerate closing timelines. The result is significantly faster reconciliation cycles, reduced billing errors, and improved financial transparency across advertising operations. The operational benefits for media businesses are substantial. By automating traditionally manual processes enterprises can eliminate administrative backlogs that often delay campaign execution, reporting and billing. AI Agents.title: Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models - Anadolu Ajansı
AI Agents.titleZh: Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models - Anadolu Ajansı
AI Agents.content: Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models [World](/en/world) # Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models Anthropic reports 80% of Claude's code is self-generated and projects future models will continuously develop their own successors despite growing warnings Abdulkadir Günyol 09 June 2026•Update: 09 June 2026 Growing calls around the world to slow or halt the development of artificial intelligence (AI) are colliding with rapid advances that are bringing the technology closer to developing future versions of itself with limited human involvement. US technology firm Anthropic recently added to those concerns by publishing a report suggesting the threshold for autonomous AI development may be closer than previously believed. The company said more than 80% of the code powering its Claude chatbot is now generated by the model itself, compared with a share in the single digits in early 2025. According to Anthropic's AI development framework, current technology has reached the fourth of five stages, where models can autonomously execute code and delegate tasks to other systems. The fifth and final stage is expected to involve autonomous agents capable of designing and training their own models, potentially allowing future AI systems to continuously develop successor versions. The developments come despite repeated warnings from academics, technology leaders and public figures about the risks associated with increasingly advanced AI systems. One of the most prominent warnings came in March 2023, when the Future of Life Institute published an open letter signed by figures including Elon Musk, calling for a six-month pause on training AI systems more powerful than GPT-4. The letter warned that AI systems approaching human-level intelligence could pose significant risks, including massive job losses, widespread misinformation and a potential loss of human control over civilization. Pope Leo XIV also addressed the issue in his encyclical Magnifica Humanitas, released in May, calling for a halt to AI development and urging countries to separate the technology from military and economic competition. The scale of global investments into the sector is one of its primary obstacles in slowing the rapid development despite these global pleas for caution. Global spending on AI is projected to rise 47% year-on-year in 2026 to $2.59 trillion, before increasing to $3.49 trillion in 2027, according to a Gartner report released in May. \*Writing by Emir Yildirim in Istanbul news\_share news\_share\_description**[subscription\_contact](https://www.aa.com.tr/tr/p/abonelik-talep-formu/1001)** [subscription](https://www.aa.com.tr/tr/p/abonelik-talep-formu/1001) Previous Previous [### Amnesty warns US travel curbs could prevent thousands of fans from attending 2026 World Cup](/en/sports/amnesty-warns-us-travel-curbs-could-prevent-thousands-of-fans-from-attending-2026-world-cup/3960828) [## LATEST NEWS](/en/latest) ### [Trump says US will declare 'total victory' over Iran within 2 weeks](/en/us-israel-iran-war/trump-says-us-will-declare-total-victory-over-iran-within-2-weeks/3960871) ### [Global calls to halt AI development clash with prospect of self-improving models](/en/world/global-calls-to-halt-ai-development-clash-with-prospect-of-self-improving-models/3960870) ### [Pentagon adds Alibaba, Baidu, BYD to list of firms allegedly aiding Chinese military](/en/americas/pentagon-adds-alibaba-baidu-byd-to-list-of-firms-allegedly-aiding-chinese-military/3960869) ### [Bolivia’s president signs law giving him new emergency powers as siege enters second month](/en/americas/bolivia-s-president-signs-law-giving-him-new-emergency-powers-as-siege-enters-second-month/3960867) ### [US Senator Sanders vows to block defense bill provision integrating American, Israeli defense sectors](/en/americas/us-senator-sanders-vows-to-block-defense-bill-provision-integrating-american-israeli-defense-sec...

Audit and advisory solutions provider Fieldguide announced the launch of Field Orchestrator, touted as a [long-horizon](https://jumpcloud.com/it-index/what-is-long-horizon-planning-in-ai) agent capable of completing substantive testing workflows. Processing Content While the specific [definition](https://www.ai21.com/glossary/ai-agent/what-are-long-horizon-tasks/) can [vary](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/what-long-horizon-agent-memory-why-ai-agents-forget-fix-kanis-patel-kjf5f/), in general a long-horizon agent can be described as an AI agent that is used not for singular tasks but managing complex goals over days, weeks or months, executing thousands of individual steps while maintaining a persistent state and memory the whole time to keep it focused on its function. Purpose-built for audit and advisory to plan and execute engagements from start to finish, Field Orchestrator is said to sustain reasoning and action across the full arc of an engagement, coordinating multi-agent workflows without continuous practitioner oversight. It treats testing not as a discrete task to be done in response to specific prompts but as a continuous workflow across population analysis, sample selection, evidence collection, extraction, testing, and workpaper generation. AI Agents.content: [Latest News](/news) [## Home Affairs opens internal "conversation" on adopting three types of AI](/news/home-affairs-opens-internal-conversation-on-adopting-three-types-of-ai-626329) [## Superloop self-serve AI resolutions top 330,000 cases](/news/superloop-self-serve-ai-resolutions-top-330000-cases-626442) [## Meta accuses NSO Group of violating court order by WhatsApp spear phishing](/news/meta-accuses-nso-group-of-violating-court-order-by-whatsapp-spear-phishing-626482) [## Tarocash owner Retail Apparel Group to give HR systems an AI boost](/news/tarocash-owner-retail-apparel-group-to-give-hr-systems-an-ai-boost-626064) [## Meta accuses Australia of breaching free trade agreement](/news/meta-accuses-australia-of-breaching-free-trade-agreement-626439) * [Home](https://www.itnews.com.au) * [News](https://www.itnews.com.au/news) * [Business](https://www.itnews.com.au/business) * [Strategy](https://www.itnews.com.au/business/strategy) # Home Affairs opens internal "conversation" on adopting three types of AI By [Ry Crozier](/author/ry-crozier-149259) Jun 9 2026 6:35AM ## As CIO addresses broader prioritisation challenges. Home Affairs considers itself to be closer to the start of its adoption of AI, with the present focus on framing a department-wide “conversation” about the technology after earlier behind-the-scenes preparatory work. Speaking at the ServiceNow Federal Forum in Canberra, CIO and first assistant secretary of digital delivery services Matt Hay said Home Affairs had an interest in three forms of AI: assisted, embedded and agentic. “We’re trying to position conversations in the context of the notion of assisted AI, Copilot being an obvious example there,” Hay said. Embedded AI is more related to AI capabilities built into existing platforms, such as ServiceNow, which Home Affairs [uses for SecOps](https://www.itnews.com.au/news/home-affairs-adds-secops-to-new-cyber-risk-overhaul-619753) and [other security-oriented use cases.](https://www.itnews.com.au/news/home-affairs-digitises-authority-to-operate-process-for-ict-systems-612567) “ServiceNow is a really good example [of embedded AI]. We’re a ServiceNow user, it comes with AI capability. How do we want to unlock that?” Hay said. Lastly, the department is looking at agentic AI although it was not clear what the specific use cases for this might be. “We’re trying to frame conversations and a strategy and approach across those three [AI] domains,” Hay said. “We're very much, I think, right now at the start in the assisted space and what we can do from [a] productivity [perspective], and we've also organisationally looked at it from an operating model perspective,” he said. AI Agents.content: [Try Gloria Terminal](https://www.itsgloria.ai/) [Earn with Nexo](https://nexo.com/?utm_source=cryptobriefing&utm_campaign=cryptobriefing_button_earn_website) #### Sections [Bitcoin](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/bitcoin/) [DeFi](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/defi/) [Ethereum](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/ethereum/) [NFTs](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/nfts/) [Meme Coins](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/meme-coins/) [AI Agents](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/ai-agents/) [Regulation](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/regulation/) [Web3](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/web3/) [Business](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/business/) [Ecosystem](https://cryptobriefing.com/category/ecosystem/) [Earn with Nexo](https://nexo.com/?utm_source=cryptobriefing&utm_campaign=cryptobriefing_button_earn_website) # MIT researchers develop self-evolving AI scientists for scientific discovery A new category-theoretic framework lets AI systems rewrite their own reasoning rules, moving beyond pattern matching toward genuine scientific breakthroughs. Share [Add us on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=cryptobriefing.com "Add Crypto Briefing as your preferred source to see more of our stories on Google.") by [Editorial Team](https://cryptobriefing.com/team/editorial-team/) Most AI systems are smart the way a very fast librarian is smart. They find patterns in existing data, retrieve relevant information, and organize it neatly. What they don’t do is have an “aha” moment. They don’t realize their entire way of thinking about a problem is wrong, tear up the playbook, and start fresh with better concepts. That’s the gap MIT researchers Fiona Y. Wang and Markus J. Buehler are trying to close. Their new preprint, published May 31 on arXiv, lays out a formal mathematical framework that would allow AI systems to revise their own reasoning structures, not just optimize within the rules they were given. ## From search to genuine discovery The paper, titled “Self-Revising Discovery Systems for Science: A Categorical Framework for Agentic Artificial Intelligence,” draws a sharp line between three things that sound similar but are fundamentally different: retrieval, search, and discovery. Retrieval is looking something up. Search is exploring a known space for something new. Discovery, the hard one, means recognizing that the space itself needs to change. Advertisement The MIT framework uses a branch of mathematics called category theory to formalize this distinction. Specifically, it employs constructs known as copresheaves and provenance categories to represent how AI systems handle data and scientific claims. These aren’t just abstract decorations. They serve as the scaffolding that lets the system track where its knowledge comes from and, crucially, identify when that knowledge structure is no longer sufficient. The framework uses mathematical tools called left Kan extensions to ensure that when the AI transitions from one reasoning regime to another, the shift is formally validated. It’s not just guessing that a new approach might work. It’s proving, in a mathematical sense, that the new schema correctly extends what came before. ## Real problems, not toy demos Theoretical elegance is nice. But Wang and Buehler backed it up with two practical implementations that tackle real materials science problems. The first, called Builder/Breaker, addresses protein mechanics. Builder/Breaker uses the categorical framework to let the AI restructure its approach to these multi-scale challenges rather than just throwing more compute at a fixed model. The second implementation, CategoryScienceClaw, takes on fiber-network modeling. CategoryScienceClaw applies the self-revising framework to discover new ways of representing and reasoning about these structures. Both implementations treat data and scientific claims as what the paper calls “typed artifacts,” meaning every piece of information carries metadata about wh...

Updates to the App Intents framework enable developers to connect their apps to Siri AI capabilities like personal context understanding, app actions, and onscreen awareness to help their users get more done. New intelligence frameworks allow developers to build AI features into their apps more easily and flexibly, tapping into powerful models from Apple and others. AI Agents.content: [Finance](/category/finance/) # Perplexity planning IPO in 2028 regardless of what happens to Anthropic or OpenAI, CEO tells CNBC Published by [Global Banking & Finance Review](/author/global-banking-and-finance-review/) Posted on June 9, 2026 1 min read · Last updated: June 9, 2026 [Add as preferred source on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=globalbankingandfinance.com) [Finance](/tag/finance/) [Banking](/tag/banking/) [Markets](/tag/markets/) ## Perplexity Targets 2028 IPO Regardless of Outcomes for Anthropic or OpenAI ## Perplexity's IPO Plans and Market Context ### Perplexity's 2028 IPO Timeline June 8 (Reuters) - AI firm Perplexity is planning to go public in 2028 regardless of how the market receives the listings of Anthropic and OpenAI, CNBC reported on Monday, citing an interview with CEO Aravind Srinivas. #### CEO Aravind Srinivas' Statement "Agnostic of these two companies, we were planning for something in 2028, so that still remains the case," Srinivas told CNBC in an interview. ##### Reporting and Editorial Credits (Reporting by Natalia Bueno Rebolledo in Mexico City; Editing by Rashmi Aich) ## Key Takeaways * Perplexity is committed to going public in 2028, unaffected by Anthropic or OpenAI’s IPO moves ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2025/03/30/perplexity-ceo-denies-having-financial-issues-says-no-ipo-before-2028/?utm_source=openai)) * CEO Aravind Srinivas says the company remains financially strong, with solid funding and rising revenue ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2025/03/30/perplexity-ceo-denies-having-financial-issues-says-no-ipo-before-2028/?utm_source=openai)) * Perplexity differs from Anthropic and OpenAI by focusing on orchestration and efficiency, aiming to maximize token value per watt per user ([businesschief.com](https://businesschief.com/news/perplexity-ceo-says-orchestration-is-crucial-for-ai-firms?utm_source=openai)) ## References * [Perplexity CEO denies having financial issues, says no IPO before 2028 | TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2025/03/30/perplexity-ceo-denies-having-financial-issues-says-no-ipo-before-2028/?utm_source=openai) * [Perplexity CEO says ‘Orchestration’ is Crucial for AI Firms | Business Chief](https://businesschief.com/news/perplexity-ceo-says-orchestration-is-crucial-for-ai-firms?utm_source=openai) ## Frequently Asked Questions When is Perplexity planning to go public? Perplexity is planning to launch its IPO in 2028. Is Perplexity's IPO dependent on Anthropic or OpenAI's market performance? No, Perplexity's IPO plans are independent of Anthropic and OpenAI's listings. Who confirmed Perplexity's IPO timeline? CEO Aravind Srinivas confirmed the company's IPO plan for 2028 in an interview with CNBC. Which news outlet reported Perplexity's IPO plan? Credit...Illustrations by Yoshi Sodeoka [Skip to content](#site-content)[Skip to site index](#site-index) # They Spent Years on a Math Problem. Then They Were Scooped by A.I. Artificial intelligence is mastering the kinds of projects that have long helped to build the careers of young mathematicians. What does that mean for their future? Credit...Illustrations by Yoshi Sodeoka Supported by [SKIP ADVERTISEMENT](#after-sponsor) Listen · 13:05 min By Gregory Barber Earlier this year, Sidharth Hariharan, a graduate student in mathematics at Carnegie Mellon University, received news that sent him rushing into his adviser’s office with tears in his eyes. He had just gotten an email from Maryna Viazovska, a professor at the École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne in Switzerland and a 2022 winner of the Fields Medal, the highest honor in mathematics. For more than two years, she and Mr. Hariharan had been leading a team of six mathematicians in an effort to break down one of Dr. Viazovska’s most celebrated proofs into distinct logical steps, a task known as formalization. But hours earlier, Dr. Viazovska had received a tip-off from a colleague: They’d been scooped. Or, as she soon became fond of putting it, they’d “gotten Gaussed.” Gauss is an artificial intelligence system built by Math, Inc., a California start-up. It had taken the team’s road map for formalizing Dr. Viazovska’s result — a solution to the densest possible arrangement of eight-dimensional spheres, popularly known as the sphere-packing problem — and completed it in just five days. While A.I. is popularly associated with stumbles in arithmetic (like ChatGPT’s [meme-worthy failure](https://8cmbykf0.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Fcommunity.openai.com%2Ft%2Fincorrect-count-of-r-characters-in-the-word-strawberry%2F829618/1/0100019e64896465-dd9e3a2c-7565-4b24-98b4-d26b04fcfe05-000000/KZUPQacRTCqm-6J285D9gYIqpSA=473) to correctly count the number of R’s in the word strawberry), technology companies have poured vast resources into reasoning systems that can solve open math problems.Recent progress has turned into an arms race between A.I. competitors eager to show off machine intelligence through success in a field that is often held up as the pinnacle of human intellect. --- Thank you for your patience while we verify access. LLMs.content: ### Topics [Biotech & Health](/category/biotech-health/) [Cloud Computing](/tag/cloud-computing/) [Commerce](/category/commerce/) [Crypto](/category/cryptocurrency/) [Enterprise](/category/enterprise/) [EVs](/tag/evs/) [Fintech](/category/fintech/) [Fundraising](/category/fundraising/) [Gadgets](/category/gadgets/) [Gaming](/category/gaming/) [Google](/tag/google/) [Government & Policy](/category/government-policy/) [Hardware](/category/hardware/) [Instagram](/tag/instagram/) [Layoffs](/tag/layoffs/) [Media & Entertainment](/category/media-entertainment/) [Meta](/tag/meta/) [Microsoft](/tag/microsoft/) [Privacy](/category/privacy/) [Robotics](/category/robotics/) [Security](/category/security/) [Social](/category/social/) [Space](/category/space/) [Startups](/category/startups/) [TikTok](/tag/tiktok/) [Transportation](/category/transportation/) [Venture](/category/venture/) ### More from TechCrunch [Staff](/about-techcrunch/) [Events](/events/) [Startup Battlefield](/startup-battlefield/) [StrictlyVC](https://strictlyvc.com/) [Newsletters](/newsletters/) [Podcasts](/podcasts/) [Videos](/video/) [Partner Content](/sponsored/) [TechCrunch Brand Studio](/brand-studio/) [Crunchboard](https://www.crunchboard.com/) [Contact Us](/contact-us/) [Apps](https://techcrunch.com/category/apps/) # NotebookLM’s new update will help you build source repository from chat [Ivan Mehta](https://techcrunch.com/author/ivan-mehta/) Google on Monday announced an update to its [NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google/) research tool, which includes new features and the shift to Gemini 3.5 as the default model. The company is also adding [Antigravity](https://antigravity.google/)-powered software skills to help users with research and generating different kinds of outputs. This is similar to Google adding coding chops to its [search](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/19/google-search-as-you-know-it-is-over/) products to make them more engaging for Q&A. Google said that with the latest update, you can start a chat about a project with the app, and it will help you build the knowledge base by suggesting different sources using its research skills and Google Search. The feature could help users get primary sources in other languages or find new material from related authors. Previously, NotebookLM required you to bring your own sources to start building a knowledge base in order to extract insights. The company added that users can now give detailed instructions to NotebookLM to generate output in different formats. Plus, they can edit the output once generated. The tool now supports exports in formats including Data visualizations and charts (.png, .svg); Documents (PDFs, .docx, Markdown, text files); Nano-banana-powered images; Structured data (.csv, .json); and Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint. Last year, the company introduced [a “Deep Research” mode for structured online research](https://techcrunch.com/2025/11/13/googles-notebooklm-adds-deep-research-tool-support-for-more-file-types/). With the new feature rollout, NotebookLM will show detailed steps in the chat about how it arrived at the answers for users to check the output. Google said that the updates are available to Google AI Ultra users and all Workspace business customers with AI Ultra Access and AI Expanded Access starting today, with the aim of expanding them to others. He is based out of India and has previously worked at publications including Huffington Post and The Next Web. You can contact or verify outreach from Ivan by emailing [im@ivanmehta.com](mailto:im@ivanmehta.com)...

LLMs.content: * [ai atlas](/ai-atlas/) * [Tech](/tech/) * Services & Software * [AI](/tech/services-and-software/ai/) # NotebookLM Is Getting Google's Latest Gemini AI Model The AI-powered research assistant is getting major chat upgrades, better customization and more formatting options. Macy Meyer Reporter II Macy is a writer on the AI Team. She covers how AI is changing daily life and how to make the most of it. This includes writing about consumer AI products and their real-world impact, from breakthrough tools reshaping daily life to the intimate ways people interact with AI technology day-to-day. Macy is a North Carolina native who graduated from UNC-Chapel Hill with a BA in English and a second BA in Journalism. You can reach her at mmeyer@cnet.com. Expertise Macy covers consumer AI products and their real-world impact Credentials * Macy has been working for CNET for coming on 2 years. Prior to CNET, Macy received a North Carolina College Media Association award in sports writing. [See full bio](/profiles/macymeyer/) Macy Meyer 2 min read Millions of people have turned to [NotebookLM](/tech/services-and-software/notebooklm-review-bring-your-own-sources-to-this-ultrapractical-google-ai-tool/) since it launched three years ago for studying, synthesizing and summarizing documents, and data organization. Now, NotebookLM is getting a big upgrade to become an even better research assistant. Google said its AI product will gain "new agentic capabilities in chat and more advanced reasoning" to handle even more complex research tasks and projects. Below, I'll break down the new updates coming to NotebookLM, rolling out globally now to Google AI Ultra subscribers and Workspace business customers with AI Ultra access. **Read also:** [Gemini Gets New Notebooks Feature That Syncs With NotebookLM](/tech/services-and-software/gemini-gets-new-notebooks-feature-that-syncs-with-notebooklm/) ## Chat gets a major upgrade NotebookLM's chat tool now runs on [Gemini 3.5](/tech/services-and-software/google-gemini-3-5-flash-spark-antigravity/) and Antigravity to improve accuracy and give clearer visibility into its reasoning and outputs, which Google said was a highly requested feature. Each notebook includes a secure cloud computer, so NotebookLM can write and run code for deeper research and analysis. NotebookLM also has more than 100 curated software skills to expand its thinking steps and allow it to create a wider range of results. ## More formatting options NotebookLM can now output answers in a wider variety of ways. The AI tool will assemble context from your sources into downloadable, editable artifacts, including data visualizations (png, svg), documents (PDF, DOCX, Markdown, text), images via Nano Banana (png, jpg, gif), structured data (CSV, JSON) and Microsoft formats (XLSX, PPTX). Google plans for more output formats to come in the future. ## Research gets easier and more trustworthy Getting started with research is easier. With the upgraded NotebookLM, you no longer need a fully formed repository of sources. NotebookLM can help you build one from loose ideas, guide source discovery inside chat and use Google Search to surface high‑quality web sources. You can also choose which sources to add to your research, and each source stays clearly attributed, so results remain grounded in information you trust. **Read also:** [NotebookLM's Video Overviews Just Got Better Thanks to a Trifecta of Google's AI Models](/tech/services-and-software/notebooklm-video-overviews-better-trifecta-google-ai-models/) These changes to NotebookLM aim to broaden real‑world use cases. For instance, a researcher can merge messy international datasets, run code to analyze them and produce charts plus a PDF report; technical teams can convert dense specs into simplified guides and slide decks; and small business owners can combine sales and spending data, then use NotebookLM's analysis and reporting to inform expansion decisions. All of these changes combine to help you mak...

You can start your research directly in a chat with NotebookLM. by Emma Roth Image: Google Emma Roth is a news writer who covers the streaming wars, consumer tech, crypto, social media, and much more. Previously, she was a writer and editor at MUO. Google is rolling out “across the board” updates to NotebookLM. The AI-powered note-taking app now uses Google’s upgraded [Gemini 3.5 model](/tech/933699/google-gemini-redesign-ai-3-5-flash-io-2026), which will allow it to respond with “more accurate and reliable information,” according [to a blog post on Monday](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/products/notebooklm/better-research-notebooklm/). [Launched in 2023](/23845856/google-notebooklm-tailwind-ai-notes-research), NotebookLM allows you to interact with your notes and sources using AI, as well as ask questions about the materials. With this update, Google says you can start a research project by just asking NotebookLM questions about a topic, instead of importing notes or YouTube videos. NotebookLM will use Google Search to help you find relevant sources, [building on its “discover” feature](/news/642490/google-notebooklm-discover-sources-ai-audio-overviews) to help you find useful resources on the web. *You can choose which sources found by NotebookLM to import.* Image: Google ### Related * [NotebookLM can now summarize research in ‘cinematic’ video overviews](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/889475/notebooklm-can-now-summarize-research-in-cinematic-video-overviews) Additionally, NotebookLM now runs on Google’s agentic coding platform, Antigravity. Each notebook within the app is connected to a “secure cloud computer,” enabling NotebookLM to write and run code to help with your research. NotebookLM can also output information in new formats and file types, including PDF documents, data visualizations (PNG, SVG), Nano Banana-generated images (PNG, JPG, GIF), Excel and PowerPoint files, CSV sheets, and more. This update is coming to users on Google’s AI Ultra plan and Workspace customers, but the company plans to expand it to more plans in the future. **Follow topics and authors** from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates. * Emma Roth ## Most Popular Most Popular 1. LLMs.title: EV maker's self-driving chief makes bold claim about rival technology - Yahoo Autos
LLMs.titleZh: EV maker's self-driving chief makes bold claim about rival technology - Yahoo Autos
LLMs.content: [Mail](https://mail.yahoo.com/) Advertisement Advertisement Advertisement Advertisement # EV maker's self-driving chief makes bold claim about rival technology [The Cool Down](https://profiles.yahoo.com/brands/the-cool-down/) James Nanzo [Add us on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=autos.yahoo.com) There are plenty of ways to earn press attention in the fraught world of autonomous driving. Distilling a brand's philosophy into a phrase like "language is poison" is certainly one way to go about it. Xpeng's head of autonomous driving, Dr. Xianming Liu, continues to draw attention for his go-to phrase in a [wide-ranging interview with Electrek](https://electrek.co/2026/06/05/xpeng-vla-interview-cvpr-language-poison-tesla-fsd/) that also covered the brand's lofty artificial intelligence budget. ### What happened? In the interview, Liu claimed that Xpeng has quickly caught up to Tesla's Full Self-Driving v13, helped by an AI training budget of roughly $41 million a month. Advertisement Advertisement Liu later clarified his famous "language is poison" phrase in the Electrek interview — Xpeng still takes language input from drivers, but it no longer wants the vehicle to produce language-like representations during driving. As Electrek described it, the updated setup skips the extra step of turning what the car sees into language-style tokens before converting that into driving commands. Liu told Electrek that removing that middle step reduces computational waste and latency. He also said Xpeng is pairing that approach with a "world model" meant to anticipate what is about to happen on the road, with a production rollout expected later this year. Liu said Xpeng's main driving stack is vision-only, though the company still includes radar and ultrasonic sensors on some vehicles for separate emergency safety systems such as automatic braking and steering. Advertisement Advertisement That puts Xpeng somewhere between Tesla's camera-only philosophy and Waymo's more sensor-heavy approach, the publication noted. Liu also touted Chinese roads as a superior training ground for autonomous driving. "In China, you have a larger chance to get corner cases and data," Liu told Electrek. "This is one advantage." ### Why does it matter? Xpeng's spending shows how much money automakers are pouring into software alongside factories and batteries. How companies best deploy their money for safety purposes is somewhat up for debate. As Electrek alluded to, Waymo and Tesla are at [opposite ends](https://www.reyeslaw.com/blog/tesla-vs-waymo-robotaxi-safety-cameras-vs-lidar/) of the camera vs. sensor debate. Advertisement Advertisement Xpeng offers another path, using the sensors as a fail-safe to help prevent some of the high-profile incidents that have plagued Tesla. "You're not talking about chatting with ChatGPT and making a mistake — you just say, 'hey, this is so silly, redo,'" Liu told Electrek. "You're talking about lives." ### What are people saying? Liu has some personal skin in the game when it comes to matching Tesla's FSD. There's a now-public bet that he'd have to streak across the Golden Gate Bridge if Xpeng failed to meet its Tesla benchmark, as Electrek noted. "I'm pretty confident I don't need to run," he told the outlet. "Based on test drives, we already hit the goal." Advertisement Advertisement He's also pouring a ton of resources into the AI systems and capitalizing on China as a prime training ground. A commenter on Electrek [opined](https://www.openweb.com/share/3Ep6UmKZ1GujWfZOdUXUtNYbhnT) that Liu "was simply being humble" in his comparisons to Tesla. "I've watched the videos — Xpeng VLA2.0 is well ahead of Tesla Assisted Driving in China," they wrote. "I expect Xpeng to leapfrog Tesla quickly when the software update is released in Europe." A commenter on Electrek [opined](https://www.openweb.com/share/3Ep6UmKZ1GujWfZOdUXUtNYbhnT) that Liu "was simply being humble" in his comparisons to Tesla. [Become an Insider](/subscription) and start reading now. Have an account? . There are far better ways to encourage [AI use than tokenmaxxing](https://www.businessinsider.com/ai-use-tracking-jpmorgan-meta-kpmg-employees-tokenmaxxing-2026-5), says Legora's chief technology officer. "A lot of people, say, [get a leaderboard](https://www.businessinsider.com/tokenmaxxing-ai-token-leaderboards-debate-2026-4) and bring up token usage at performance reviews," said Jacob Lauritzen on an episode of the "20VC" podcast released on Saturday. "That leads to tokenmaxing, which is people just burn tokens just to look good." "That's a really stupid way to do anything," he added. Tokenmaxxing refers to using tons of AI tools like Claude, Codex, and Cursor to boost productivity and get ahead on internal AI use dashboards and reviews. Lauritzen, who joined the legal AI startup in 2024, said that more intelligent ways to use AI include hack days or demos where employees can show others what they're building and the efficiency gains they have achieved. "Reward them for being effective and efficient and having more output, not for necessarily using AI," he said. That said, Lauritzen added that fast-growing companies like Legora have a lot to lose when they don't use AI. [### Explore BI Games Take a smarter break in your day - and see how far you get. Play now](/games) "Is it worth us spending a ton of tokens to learn if it maybe gives us 20% efficiency for us? Yes, we have a really high opportunity cost," he said. Lauritzen's comments come at a pivotal moment for the tech industry, as it moves from tokenmaxxing to [token capping](https://www.businessinsider.com/token-reckoning-amazon-uber-reassess-ai-investments-2026-6). Some tech companies are wondering if the dashboards they implemented as motivation to play around with AI are backfiring — and finance departments are increasingly concerned about how much it all costs. Last week, Uber said it has limited all employees to $1,500 in monthly token spend per AI tool, after the ride-hailing company blew through its AI spend budget earlier this year. Last month, the Financial Times reported that [Amazon shuttered an internal dashboard](https://www.businessinsider.com/amazon-ai-leaderboard-tokenmaxxing-2026-5) that tracked AI use after some staff performed tasks to climb the leaderboard. An Amazon spokesperson told Business Insider that the unofficial dashboard "was never intended to promote the use of AI for usage's sake." At a Bloomberg conference last week, Andrew Feldman, the CEO of Cerebras Systems, said that the idea of giving employees unlimited tokens was "boneheaded from the get-go....

As the fervor of [tokenmaxxing dies down](https://www.businessinsider.com/tokenmaxxing-debate-uber-exec-viral-ai-costs-2026-5), some AI users are wondering how to get more bang for their buck and keep their monthly costs in check. Coinbase CEO [Brian Armstrong](https://www.businessinsider.com/coinbase-layoffs-ai-brian-armstrong-job-cuts-letter-2026-5) shared the crypto company's strategy: not skimping on the cheaper models. "We're working hard on routing prompts to cheaper models where appropriate, and in some cases have been able to keep costs roughly flat, while token usage continues to grow exponentially," Armstrong wrote [on X](https://x.com/brian_armstrong/status/2063782620815876515?s=20) on Sunday. While the latest models like Opus 4.8 or GPT-5.5 promise bleeding-edge benefits, they can also devour more tokens. (That's before you turn on Fast mode.) When Anthropic [launchedi Opus 4.7](https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-claude-opus-4-7-backlash-tokens-2026-4), many users complained that they were quickly hitting rate limits. Armstrong wrote that he anticipated "80% of workloads will be running on 99% cheaper models within 12-18 months." The only times when users will use the latest models, Armstrong predicted, are when they need to be "IQ maxing." This includes scientific breakthroughs or agent orchestration. "This leads me to think the limiting factor will be energy and compute, not better models," Armstrong wrote. [### Explore BI Games Take a smarter break in your day - and see how far you get. Play now](/games) The Coinbase CEO's post caught the attention of some tech luminaries. Venture capitalist Marc Andreessen [called it](https://x.com/pmarca/status/2063798598068846984?s=20) "interesting." Hugging Face cofounder Julien Chaumond [wrote that](https://x.com/julien_c/status/2063905239690788957?s=20) "model routing is growing a lot these days." Box CEO [Aaron Levie](https://www.businessinsider.com/box-ceo-monitors-ai-power-slack-channel-not-tokenmaxxing-leaderboard-2026-4) wrote that Armstrong's numbers were a "bit extreme," but that AI use would [likely stratify](https://x.com/levie/status/2063835799096090749?s=20) in the coming years. "High end" work will be completed by leading models, Levie wrote, while "high volume" work will be relegated to the cheap models. "Intelligence allocation is going to be extremely important," Harvey cofounder [Winston Weinberg](https://x.com/winstonweinberg/status/2063892870289879240?s=20) wrote. The ef...

The only times when users will use the latest models, Armstrong predicted, are when they need to be "IQ maxing." This includes scientific breakthroughs or agent orchestration. "This leads me to think the limiting factor will be energy and compute, not better models," Armstrong wrote. [### Explore BI Games Take a smarter break in your day - and see how far you get. Play now](/games) The Coinbase CEO's post caught the attention of some tech luminaries. Venture capitalist Marc Andreessen [called it](https://x.com/pmarca/status/2063798598068846984?s=20) "interesting." Hugging Face cofounder Julien Chaumond [wrote that](https://x.com/julien_c/status/2063905239690788957?s=20) "model routing is growing a lot these days." Box CEO [Aaron Levie](https://www.businessinsider.com/box-ceo-monitors-ai-power-slack-channel-not-tokenmaxxing-leaderboard-2026-4) wrote that Armstrong's numbers were a "bit extreme," but that AI use would [likely stratify](https://x.com/levie/status/2063835799096090749?s=20) in the coming years. "High end" work will be completed by leading models, Levie wrote, while "high volume" work will be relegated to the cheap models. "Intelligence allocation is going to be extremely important," Harvey cofounder [Winston Weinberg](https://x.com/winstonweinberg/status/2063892870289879240?s=20) wrote. The ef...
