AI Product.content: [Skip to main content](#main-content) [Axios](/) 6 mins ago - [Business](https://www.axios.com/business) # Axios C-Suite: 3 new AI developments for the week of June 6 * [Andrew Kay](https://www.axios.com/authors/andrew.kay@axios.com) [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) Add Axios as your preferred source to see more of our stories on Google. [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) **1. Fast action AI software:** OpenAI [launched Sites](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly9kZXZlbG9wZXJzLm9wZW5haS5jb20vY29kZXgvc2l0ZXM_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uZXdzbGV0dGVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPW5ld3NsZXR0ZXJfY3N1aXRlX2JldGEmc3RyZWFtPXRvcA/586d9e571e560373298b467cBc1d0f48f), a Codex feature that lets your staff create, host and deploy work-related web apps from prompts internally — instantly. * **Why it matters:** Custom software usually requires vendor contracts, engineering time or a long IT queue. Non-engineers can skip all that and build, ship and USE internal tools themselves. **2. AI's cheaper future:** Nvidia's RTX Spark and Microsoft's Project Solara [aim to move](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDUvMzAvbnZpZGlhLW1pY3Jvc29mdC1wY3MtYWktc3VyZmFjZS1kZWxsP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9bmV3c2xldHRlciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1uZXdzbGV0dGVyX2NzdWl0ZV9iZXRhJnN0cmVhbT10b3A/586d9e571e560373298b467cBe2976078) AI inference from the cloud onto personal devices. * **Why it matters:** Companies are starting to ration AI [as token costs rise](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDUvMTgvdG9rZW5zLWFpLXNvZnR3YXJlLWNvc3QtcHJlcC1jZW8_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uZXdzbGV0dGVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPW5ld3NsZXR0ZXJfY3N1aXRlX2JldGEmc3RyZWFtPXRvcA/586d9e571e560373298b467cBbf685d8d). Local [AI](https://www.axios.com/technology/automation-and-ai) will make usage less metered, like a shift from paying by the minute for long-distance calls to an unlimited plan. **3. Physical AI's GPT moment:** Nvidia [unveiled Cosmos 3](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDYvMDEvbnZpZGlhLWFpLXB1c2gtY29zbW9zLTMtd29ybGQtbW9kZWw_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uZXdzbGV0dGVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPW5ld3NsZXR0ZXJfY3N1aXRlX2JldGEmc3RyZWFtPXRvcA/586d9e571e560373298b467cB18c5392a), a foundation model for physical AI. * **Why it matters:** The first wave of AI read and wrote. The next has to see, move and react to power a factory robot reaching for a part. Trained on physics rather than text, Cosmos 3 aims to be the infrastructure for that shift. 📈 *If you're a CEO or on a CEO's team: [Ask to join](https://www.axios.com/newsletters/c-suite?utm_medium=native&utm_source=axios_article&utm_campaign=csuite) Jim's new weekly Axios C-Suite newsletter.* [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) ##### What to read next

AI Product.titleZh: Apple's Image Playground Gets 'Photorealistic' Capabilities With New AI - CNET
AI Product.content: * [ai atlas](/ai-atlas/) * [apple event](/apple-event/) * [Tech](/tech/) * Services & Software * [AI](/tech/services-and-software/ai/) # Apple's Image Playground Gets 'Photorealistic' Capabilities With New AI At WWDC 2026, Apple shows off new AI image creation and editing tools. Katelyn Chedraoui Reporter 2 Katelyn is a reporter with CNET covering artificial intelligence, including chatbots, image and video generators. Her work explores how new AI technology is infiltrating our lives, shaping the content we consume on social media and affecting the people behind the screens. She graduated from the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill with a degree in media and journalism. You can reach her at kchedraoui@cnet.com. Expertise artificial intelligence, AI image generators, social media platforms [See full bio](/profiles/kchedraoui/) Katelyn Chedraoui Apple's creative AI hub Image Playground will be capable of creating "photorealistic" AI images, thanks to new AI models running behind the scenes. Apple announced the news at [WWDC 2026](https://www.cnet.com/news-live/wwdc-2026-apple-live-news-updates/), the company's annual developers conference. Image Playground is one of Apple's original AI hubs, where you can create images with generative AI. Now, that content should be more customizable and look less like plastic AI slop. You will be able to edit specific parts of an image -- an important capability for error-prone AI -- by tapping on it and describing the change you want with a simple text prompt. You'll be able to use these AI creations across your device, including to make contact posters and backgrounds. We're [getting our first look](https://www.cnet.com/tech/services-and-software/apple-wwdc-2026-how-to-watch-what-we-expect/) at the next generation of Apple software, including iOS 27. It's also Tim Cook's last event as CEO, with hardware chief John Ternus expected to step up into the top role before its September iPhone 18 event. Every major tech company has become extremely invested in AI. Apple isn't an exception, but it has taken a more measured approach since launching its AI two years ago. That's all changing today as the company is expected to unveil its most significant updates to Apple Intelligence yet, specifically in the form of a new-and-improved Siri. Her work explores how new AI technology is infiltrating our lives, shaping the content we consume on social media and affecting the people behind the screens. She graduated from the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill with a degree in media and journalism. You can reach her at kchedraoui@cnet.com. [See full bio](/profiles/kchedraoui/)

OpenAI has filed its confidential S-1, the first official step toward a [blockbuster IPO](https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-submits-s-1-joins-ipo-race-with-openai-2026-6). The ChatGPT-maker announced the filing on Monday in a short, blunt blog post. The confidential S-1 draft gives the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission time to review OpenAI's plans for an eventual [public listing](https://www.businessinsider.com/spacex-ipo-spcx-stock-anthropic-openai-fast-entry-sp500-inclusion-2026-6) — though the post made it sound like that could be a ways out. "We expect it to leak so we're just announcing it," [OpenAI](https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-ipo-racing-to-go-public-2026-5)'s post said. "We have not decided on timing yet; it may be a while because there are things we want to do that are likely easier as a private company." It continued: "But it's a complicated set of tradeoffs and this gives us the option to go public sooner if that ends up being best." *This is a developing story.* ## Read next ### Business Insider tells the innovative stories you want to know ### Business Insider tells the innovative stories you want to know ### Business Insider tells the innovative stories you want to know ### Business Insider tells the innovative stories you want to know [Stephen Council](https://www.businessinsider.com/author/stephen-council) You're currently following this author! AI Product.content: * [About us](https://www.exchangewire.com/about-us/) * [Contact Us](https://www.exchangewire.com/contact/) * [Homepage](https://www.exchangewire.com/ "Homepage") * [Agentic AI](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/agentic-ai/ "Agentic AI") * ExchangeWire PressBox in [Agentic AI](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/agentic-ai/)[Media](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/media/)[Revenue](https://www.exchangewire.com/blog/category/revenue/) 1 hour ago # ADvendio Launches Specialised AI Workforce to Drive Agentic Advertising **[ADvendio](https://www.advendio.com?utm_source=chatgpt.com), a leading revenue OS for agentic advertising announced today (8th June, 2026) the launch of a new category of agentic AI that marks the first time publishers, broadcasters, and retail media networks will have access to agents that are built specifically for the advertising industry.** To date, advertising teams have been reliant on AI chatbots with limited scope and understanding of this specific market. Now, the launch of the first Specialised Agentic Workforce signals a new era of autonomous co-workers capable of executing workflows and scaling operations with minimal manual intervention. ADvendio’s Specialised Agentic Workforce is different from other agents as it has been designed and built from the ground up for media operations environments. This is important in particular as it increases the value and usefulness of the tasks these agents can handle, allowing team leaders to delegate operations from advertising sales to campaign execution with higher confidence. Powered by AdCP, these digital co-workers can instantly co-ordinate with each other and existing enterprise systems to streamline the entire advertising lifecycle. "The advertising industry has lacked access to a unique agentic AI solution that can actually perform high-value tasks for too long," said Julian Ahrends, CTO at ADvendio. "With increasing pressure to grow revenue across omnichannel campaigns that grow more complex by the day, we’re proud that the Specialised Agentic Workforce from ADvendio is being introduced at a pivotal moment for advertising and media leaders, bringing advanced autonomous capabilities directly into our AdOne, AdFinance, AdGateway, and AdPortal solutions." The launch introduces an initial suite of nine specialised AI agent roles, strategically grouped across three core pillars of media operations—sales, ad ops, and finance—to eliminate systemic industry bottlenecks. To accelerate sales, the seller agent acts as an always-on, profit-optimised sales rep to scale client acquisition; the sales-enablement agent provides critical insights to close deals faster; the proposal agent instantly creates proposals to maintain pipeline momentum; and the campaign rebooking agent clones successful campaigns for repeat revenue. For ad ops, the targeting agent pinpoints ideal audience segments and inventory to hit campaign goals; the optimisation agent autonomously adjusts budgets and shifts spend across omnichannel environments to maximise campaign yield; and the inventory agent allows internal teams to check availability and get real-time delivery updates via a simple chat interface. Finally, the finance suite streamlines back-office administration: the invoicing agent executes precise billing runs instantly with a single command, while the month-end agent autonomously organises line items and cross-references delivery data against contract terms to drastically accelerate closing timelines. The result is significantly faster reconciliation cycles, reduced billing errors, and improved financial transparency across advertising operations. The operational benefits for media businesses are substantial. By automating traditionally manual processes enterprises can eliminate administrative backlogs that often delay campaign execution, reporting and billing. Workflows that previously required days or weeks of coordination can now be completed in seconds thro...

AI Product.title: OpenAI and Anthropic have officially started the IPO race - PitchBook
AI Product.titleZh: OpenAI and Anthropic have officially started the IPO race - PitchBook
AI Product.content: # OpenAI and Anthropic have officially started the IPO race ## Both companies have now disclosed that they recently submitted draft S-1 documents to regulators. With [OpenAI](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/149504-14)‘s announcement Monday that—like Anthropic—it has [confidentially filed](https://openai.com/index/openai-submits-confidential-s-1/) for an IPO with the SEC, the race for the mega-AI lab IPO is officially on. The ChatGPT-maker’s announcement is scant on details, with a blog post warning that it has not committed to any timing for the IPO, and that “it may be a while because there are things we want to do that are likely easier as a private company.” Anthropic’s announcement last week was equally light, noting that its “proposed initial public offering will depend on market conditions and other factors.” Still, the fact that both companies have taken this step—getting draft versions of their IPO prospectuses into regulators’ hands for feedback—means they want to be ready to pull the trigger at any time, whether it be now or several quarters from now. Ensuring their IPO documents have the blessing of the SEC will give them the luxury to base their timing decisions on other factors. After SpaceX’s historically large IPO, which is set to take place later this week, all eyes will turn to the two AI giants as they take their rivalry to the public markets. And as PitchBook’s Kyle Stanford, director of US VC research, [has previously posited](https://pitchbook.com/news/articles/the-mega-ipos-that-could-shut-out-the-rest-of-vc), these three companies have the potential to break the IPO gates fully open or swallow the whole market and shut it closed for other candidates. Notably, after initial floating plans to go public at a $2 trillion valuation, SpaceX last week announced a target of $1.8 trillion. Through their most recent equity funding rounds, OpenAI and Anthropic currently have $852 billion and $965 billion post-money valuations, respectively. AI Product.content: [Skip to content](#main-content) [LDS Let's Data ScienceLEARN • BUILD • STAY AHEAD](/) [Sign in](/login?redirect=%2Fnews%2Fapple-debuts-integrated-ai-features-at-wwdc-f3115011) Products & Toolsapplesirigoogle geminiai integration # Apple Debuts Integrated AI Features at WWDC | 7.0 Relevance Score Apple unveiled a major set of AI features at its developer conference, described by TechCrunch as the company's "biggest AI launch to date," including automated capabilities embedded across its software and a partnership with Google Gemini for some functionality. TechCrunch quoted Craig Federighi saying Apple wants to "turn the potential of advanced technology into helpful and intuitive products for everyone." The New York Times reported that Apple is expected to reintroduce a long-delayed Siri upgrade and said Apple Intelligence arrived later than planned and previously suffered errors that led the company to disable a feature. Reporting frames the moves as Apple integrating AI into its ecosystem rather than reorganizing around the technology. ### What happened Apple unveiled a broad set of AI features at its annual developer conference, which TechCrunch called the company's "biggest AI launch to date." TechCrunch reported the release embeds new automated capabilities across Apple's software and noted a partnership leveraging Google Gemini for some features. The New York Times reported that Apple is expected to reintroduce a previously delayed Siri upgrade and that Apple Intelligence originally arrived later than expected and produced errors that led Apple to disable a feature. ### Technical details Editorial analysis - technical context: Public reporting indicates Apple is focusing on integrating AI into the user experience and ecosystem rather than a wholesale reorganization around AI. Industry-pattern observations: companies pursuing similar integrations commonly mix on-device processing and cloud-hosted large models to balance latency, capability, and privacy trade-offs; relying on third-party models via APIs can accelerate feature rollout but introduces dependencies for updates, pricing, and model behavior. ### Context and significance TechCrunch frames Apple's messaging at WWDC as a response to narrative that the company lagged in AI, and quoted Craig Federighi arguing for a people-first approach to AI. The New York Times contrasted Apple's approach with rival Big Tech firms that have reorganized or invested heavily in AI as a standalone business priority. Observers noted the move emphasizes Apple's existing ecosystem and device-centric distribution model rather than attempting to compete purely on foundational-model scale. ### What to watch * •Adoption signals: user engagement with the new Siri features and Apple Intelligence in iOS and macOS, as reported by usage metrics or developer feedback. * •Integration trade-offs: whether future updates shift functionality between on-device components and cloud-backed models, and how that affects latency and privacy claims. * •Ecosystem effects: how Apple's reliance on Google Gemini-powered capabilities influences developer APIs, third-party integrations, and competitive differentiation. ## Scoring Rationale A major, mainstream product company integrating AI across its OS matters to practitioners because it shapes developer priorities, distribution, and privacy/latency trade-offs. The story is notable but not a frontier-model release. Practice interview problems based on real data 1,500+ SQL & Python problems across 15 industry datasets — the exact type of data you work with. Open Source AI.titleZh: From Prompt to Pipeline: Securing the AI-Powered DevOps Stack - Dark Reading
Open Source AI.content: * [Application Security](/application-security) # From Prompt to Pipeline: Securing the AI-Powered DevOps Stack Learn how to orchestrate multi-layered defenses across your endpoints, API gateways, and Git hosting platforms to counter emerging AI threat vectors. June 8, 2026 5 Min Read SOURCE: JUST\_SUPER / GETTY IMAGES Sponsored by Xopero Autonomous AI agents are now staple components of modern DevOps pipelines, enhancing coding productivity and automating tasks. Security teams are struggling to safeguard AI workflows because it requires coordination and setup at different levels and touchpoints. To ensure safety, however, this multi-step hardening must be accomplished. Furthermore, AI threat vectors are accelerating rapidly. According to the [2026 DevOps Threats Unwrapped Report](https://gitprotect.io/devops-threats-unwrapped-2026.html) by GitProtect, in 2025 alone, popular Git hosting platforms experienced 68 AI-related issues of varied impact. And the number was growing each quarter. ## Weaponizing the Assistants and Poisoning the Pipeline AI agents can interact with your DevOps pipeline just like humans, leveraging tokens, API keys, and permissions. Operating at machine speed and scale, they can threaten your most precious assets in several highly dangerous ways: ### Direct Prompt Injection Your user’s credentials or token are abused or stolen. An attacker injects malicious prompts. Equipped with robust permissions, an AI agent performs malicious actions one by one, letting the attacker bypass data-loss prevention (DLP) mechanisms, overwrite IDE configuration, dump proprietary code (intellectual property), or exfiltrate sensitive credentials. Related:[Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover](/application-security/coding-gaffe-exposes-microsoft-365-accounts-takeover) ### Indirect Prompt Injection Malicious prompts are ingested from a source that one of your developers added to an AI agent, using the MCP server technology, e.g., an open-source repo, a Jira ticket, a file. Results? The same as above. ### Remote Code Execution Following a malicious injection or vulnerability exploitation, an attacker can abuse or destroy your environment from the outside. ### Supply Chain Exploits A dev installs an extension from a marketplace, which had been compromised in its official repo. An attacker can use it for arbitrary command execution. ### AI Context Window Poisoning A malicious actor sabotages your organization's repos by committing subtly flawed code or poisoned training data for subsequent reuse by AI agents and other developers. ### Endpoint Shadow Logging Cyber criminals harvest the contents of unencrypted AI agent logs to extract, for example, API tokens. ### Blind Reliance on Hallucinations Your devs don’t revise or refactor AI-generated code, which may introduce issues and vulnerabilities. According to a CodeRabbit study, AI-authored pull requests introduce 1.7 times more problems than those created by humans. ## Securing Your Code and Business: A Multi-Dimensional Effort Securing the AI-powered DevOps stack is not a question of a single security dashboard. You need to orchestrate hardening actions across 3 complex layers. Related:[Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users](/application-security/malicious-notifications-could-trick-google-gemini-users) ### #1 The Endpoint & IDE Layer First, prevent the threat from entering the developer workstation by whitelisting DXT extensions and MDM servers that they can use with AI agents and IDE. You can use Intune (or GPO) for VS Code (and partially for Studio Code). For other IDEs, use tools like JetBrains Toolbox Enterprise or WDAC/AppLocker. A whitelisted MCP server can still be compromised (e.g., through indirect prompt injection). That’s why it’s a good idea to run MCP servers in an isolated local container runtime (e.g. local Docker or Podman sandboxes) to halt any malicious shell actions. Thirdly, secure configuration state. Malicious pr...

Open Source AI.content: * [ai atlas](/ai-atlas/) * [Tech](/tech/) * Services & Software * [AI](/tech/services-and-software/ai/) # NotebookLM Is Getting Google's Latest Gemini AI Model The AI-powered research assistant is getting major chat upgrades, better customization and more formatting options. Macy Meyer Reporter II Macy is a writer on the AI Team. She covers how AI is changing daily life and how to make the most of it. This includes writing about consumer AI products and their real-world impact, from breakthrough tools reshaping daily life to the intimate ways people interact with AI technology day-to-day. Macy is a North Carolina native who graduated from UNC-Chapel Hill with a BA in English and a second BA in Journalism. You can reach her at mmeyer@cnet.com. Expertise Macy covers consumer AI products and their real-world impact Credentials * Macy has been working for CNET for coming on 2 years. Prior to CNET, Macy received a North Carolina College Media Association award in sports writing. [See full bio](/profiles/macymeyer/) Macy Meyer 2 min read Millions of people have turned to [NotebookLM](/tech/services-and-software/notebooklm-review-bring-your-own-sources-to-this-ultrapractical-google-ai-tool/) since it launched three years ago for studying, synthesizing and summarizing documents, and data organization. Now, NotebookLM is getting a big upgrade to become an even better research assistant. Google said its AI product will gain "new agentic capabilities in chat and more advanced reasoning" to handle even more complex research tasks and projects. Below, I'll break down the new updates coming to NotebookLM, rolling out globally now to Google AI Ultra subscribers and Workspace business customers with AI Ultra access. **Read also:** [Gemini Gets New Notebooks Feature That Syncs With NotebookLM](/tech/services-and-software/gemini-gets-new-notebooks-feature-that-syncs-with-notebooklm/) ## Chat gets a major upgrade NotebookLM's chat tool now runs on [Gemini 3.5](/tech/services-and-software/google-gemini-3-5-flash-spark-antigravity/) and Antigravity to improve accuracy and give clearer visibility into its reasoning and outputs, which Google said was a highly requested feature. Each notebook includes a secure cloud computer, so NotebookLM can write and run code for deeper research and analysis. NotebookLM also has more than 100 curated software skills to expand its thinking steps and allow it to create a wider range of results. ## More formatting options NotebookLM can now output answers in a wider variety of ways. The AI tool will assemble context from your sources into downloadable, editable artifacts, including data visualizations (png, svg), documents (PDF, DOCX, Markdown, text), images via Nano Banana (png, jpg, gif), structured data (CSV, JSON) and Microsoft formats (XLSX, PPTX). Google plans for more output formats to come in the future. ## Research gets easier and more trustworthy Getting started with research is easier. With the upgraded NotebookLM, you no longer need a fully formed repository of sources. NotebookLM can help you build one from loose ideas, guide source discovery inside chat and use Google Search to surface high‑quality web sources. You can also choose which sources to add to your research, and each source stays clearly attributed, so results remain grounded in information you trust. **Read also:** [NotebookLM's Video Overviews Just Got Better Thanks to a Trifecta of Google's AI Models](/tech/services-and-software/notebooklm-video-overviews-better-trifecta-google-ai-models/) These changes to NotebookLM aim to broaden real‑world use cases. For instance, a researcher can merge messy international datasets, run code to analyze them and produce charts plus a PDF report; technical teams can convert dense specs into simplified guides and slide decks; and small business owners can combine sales and spending data, then use NotebookLM's analysis and reporting to inform expansion decisions. All of these changes combine to help you mak...

Open Source AI.content: * [ai atlas](/ai-atlas/) * [Tech](/tech/) * Services & Software * [AI](/tech/services-and-software/ai/) # OpenAI Confirms Confidential IPO Filing, With Big Stakes for the AI Boom OpenAI's move toward public markets raises a bigger question: Is the AI surge a lasting shift or an expensive gamble? Gael Cooper CNET editor Gael Fashingbauer Cooper, a journalist and pop-culture junkie, is co-author of "Whatever Happened to Pudding Pops? The Lost Toys, Tastes and Trends of the '70s and '80s," as well as "The Totally Sweet '90s." She's been a journalist since 1989, working at Mpls.St.Paul Magazine, Twin Cities Sidewalk, the Minneapolis Star Tribune, and NBC News Digital. She's Gen X in birthdate, word and deed. If Marathon candy bars ever come back, she'll be first in line. Expertise Breaking news, entertainment, lifestyle, travel, food, shopping and deals, product reviews, money and finance, video games, pets, history, books, technology history, and generational studies Credentials * Co-author of two Gen X pop-culture encyclopedia for Penguin Books. Won "Headline Writer of the Year"​ award for 2017, 2014 and 2013 from the American Copy Editors Society. Won first place in headline writing from the 2013 Society for Features Journalism. [See full bio](/profiles/gcooper/) Gael Cooper 3 min read OpenAI, perhaps the best-known company in the booming artificial intelligence market, filed confidentially on Monday for an initial public offering. Although there's no date yet for the company's public offering, this is a much-anticipated move, and [according to The New York Times,](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/08/technology/openai-ipo.html "(opens in a new window)") an OpenAI IPO "could be one of the largest public offerings to hit Wall Street." "We recently submitted a confidential S-1. We expect it to leak so we're just announcing it," OpenAI said [in a statement posted on X](https://x.com/OpenAINewsroom/status/2064094175541461220 "(opens in a new window)") on Monday afternoon. "We have not decided on timing yet; it may be a while because there are things we want to do that are likely easier as a private company. But it's a complicated set of trade-offs and this gives us the option to go public sooner if that ends up being best." Filing confidentially means that, while OpenAI has likely begun the IPO process and submitted documents to the Securities and Exchange Commission, the details remain private. It's different from a public filing, where the company's prospectus and financial information are available for investors to review. A representative for OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment. (Disclosure: Ziff Davis, CNET's parent company, in 2025 filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging it infringed Ziff Davis copyrights in training and operating its AI systems.) OpenAI was founded in 2015 by Elon Musk and current OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. (Musk left the company's board in 2018, and later sued Altman, in a trial that ended in Altman's favor [just last month](/tech/services-and-software/openai-elon-musk-ruling-court-potential-ipo/).) In 2022, the company [released ChatGPT,](/tech/services-and-software/what-is-chatgpt-everything-you-need-to-know-about-the-ai-chatbot/) a generative artificial intelligence chatbot based on large language model technology. Few apps have expanded as rapidly as ChatGPT, which amassed [hundreds of millions of users](/tech/services-and-software/what-is-chatgpt-everything-you-need-to-know-about-the-ai-chatbot/) in record time and has become, to many people, shorthand for AI chatbots. The IPO will be closely watched, as investors weigh whether [Altman's own warnings](/tech/services-and-software/openai-ceo-sam-altman-believes-were-in-an-ai-bubble/) about an AI bubble are correct. If OpenAI goes public, it would join a slate of high-profile IPOs expected this year, including Musk's SpaceX as well as Anthropic, OpenAI's major rival in artificial intelligence. The rush toward IPOs shows in part how eage...

Open Source AI.title: OpenAI and Anthropic have officially started the IPO race - PitchBook
Open Source AI.titleZh: OpenAI and Anthropic have officially started the IPO race - PitchBook
Open Source AI.content: # OpenAI and Anthropic have officially started the IPO race ## Both companies have now disclosed that they recently submitted draft S-1 documents to regulators. With [OpenAI](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/149504-14)‘s announcement Monday that—like Anthropic—it has [confidentially filed](https://openai.com/index/openai-submits-confidential-s-1/) for an IPO with the SEC, the race for the mega-AI lab IPO is officially on. The ChatGPT-maker’s announcement is scant on details, with a blog post warning that it has not committed to any timing for the IPO, and that “it may be a while because there are things we want to do that are likely easier as a private company.” Anthropic’s announcement last week was equally light, noting that its “proposed initial public offering will depend on market conditions and other factors.” Still, the fact that both companies have taken this step—getting draft versions of their IPO prospectuses into regulators’ hands for feedback—means they want to be ready to pull the trigger at any time, whether it be now or several quarters from now. Ensuring their IPO documents have the blessing of the SEC will give them the luxury to base their timing decisions on other factors. After SpaceX’s historically large IPO, which is set to take place later this week, all eyes will turn to the two AI giants as they take their rivalry to the public markets. And as PitchBook’s Kyle Stanford, director of US VC research, [has previously posited](https://pitchbook.com/news/articles/the-mega-ipos-that-could-shut-out-the-rest-of-vc), these three companies have the potential to break the IPO gates fully open or swallow the whole market and shut it closed for other candidates. Notably, after initial floating plans to go public at a $2 trillion valuation, SpaceX last week announced a target of $1.8 trillion. Through their most recent equity funding rounds, OpenAI and Anthropic currently have $852 billion and $965 billion post-money valuations, respectively. Open Source AI.content: ### Topics [Biotech & Health](/category/biotech-health/) [Cloud Computing](/tag/cloud-computing/) [Commerce](/category/commerce/) [Crypto](/category/cryptocurrency/) [Enterprise](/category/enterprise/) [EVs](/tag/evs/) [Fintech](/category/fintech/) [Fundraising](/category/fundraising/) [Gadgets](/category/gadgets/) [Gaming](/category/gaming/) [Google](/tag/google/) [Government & Policy](/category/government-policy/) [Hardware](/category/hardware/) [Instagram](/tag/instagram/) [Layoffs](/tag/layoffs/) [Media & Entertainment](/category/media-entertainment/) [Meta](/tag/meta/) [Microsoft](/tag/microsoft/) [Privacy](/category/privacy/) [Robotics](/category/robotics/) [Security](/category/security/) [Social](/category/social/) [Space](/category/space/) [Startups](/category/startups/) [TikTok](/tag/tiktok/) [Transportation](/category/transportation/) [Venture](/category/venture/) ### More from TechCrunch [Staff](/about-techcrunch/) [Events](/events/) [Startup Battlefield](/startup-battlefield/) [StrictlyVC](https://strictlyvc.com/) [Newsletters](/newsletters/) [Podcasts](/podcasts/) [Videos](/video/) [Partner Content](/sponsored/) [TechCrunch Brand Studio](/brand-studio/) [Crunchboard](https://www.crunchboard.com/) [Contact Us](/contact-us/) [Apps](https://techcrunch.com/category/apps/) # NotebookLM’s new update will help you build source repository from chat [Ivan Mehta](https://techcrunch.com/author/ivan-mehta/) Google on Monday announced an update to its [NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google/) research tool, which includes new features and the shift to Gemini 3.5 as the default model. The company is also adding [Antigravity](https://antigravity.google/)-powered software skills to help users with research and generating different kinds of outputs. This is similar to Google adding coding chops to its [search](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/19/google-search-as-you-know-it-is-over/) products to make them more engaging for Q&A. Google said that with the latest update, you can start a chat about a project with the app, and it will help you build the knowledge base by suggesting different sources using its research skills and Google Search. The feature could help users get primary sources in other languages or find new material from related authors. Previously, NotebookLM required you to bring your own sources to start building a knowledge base in order to extract insights. The company added that users can now give detailed instructions to NotebookLM to generate output in different formats. Plus, they can edit the output once generated. The tool now supports exports in formats including Data visualizations and charts (.png, .svg); Documents (PDFs, .docx, Markdown, text files); Nano-banana-powered images; Structured data (.csv, .json); and Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint. Last year, the company introduced [a “Deep Research” mode for structured online research](https://techcrunch.com/2025/11/13/googles-notebooklm-adds-deep-research-tool-support-for-more-file-types/). With the new feature rollout, NotebookLM will show detailed steps in the chat about how it arrived at the answers for users to check the output. Google said that the updates are available to Google AI Ultra users and all Workspace business customers with AI Ultra Access and AI Expanded Access starting today, with the aim of expanding them to others. Infra & Chips.title: Nvidia strikes AI cloud, memory deals in Korea - Light Reading
Infra & Chips.titleZh: Nvidia strikes AI cloud, memory deals in Korea - Light Reading
Infra & Chips.content: * [AI & Machine Learning](/digital-transformation/ai-machine-learning) * [Cloud](/digital-transformation/cloud) * [Data Centers](/it-infrastructure/data-centers) * [Network Automation](/digital-transformation/network-automation) * [News](/latest-news) # Nvidia strikes AI cloud, memory deals in Korea Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang clinches AI deals with SK Group, Naver and LG Group in Seoul, following a series of partnerships announced in Taipei last week. [Robert Clark,](/author/robert-clark)Contributing Editor,Light Reading June 9, 2026 2 Min Read (Source: Nvidia) Another week, another slew of Nvidia AI deals with Asian partners. Last week Nvidia boss Jensen Huang unveiled a series of partnerships on a visit to Taipei, including[with SKT on physical AI.](/ai-machine-learning/nvidia-taps-skt-for-physical-ai) Now he's in Seoul announcing multiple deals with Korean firms, notably with the SK Group, the parent of SK Telecom and long-time Nvidia supplier SK Hynix. The scale and breadth of these tie-ups underscore the importance of Korea not only to Nvidia's global supply chain but also as a place that can validate its tech at scale.  In a joint briefing with Huang at SK headquarters on Monday, SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won said the new agreements signaled a deeper alliance that goes beyond just memory chips, Korea Herald[reported](https://www.koreaherald.com/article/10766123). "Most of our cooperation so far has been centered on memory, but from now on, we will raise the partnership to the SK Group level," Chey said. In the most significant announcement,[SK Hynix and Nvidia struck](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/sk-hynix-ai-factory) a multi-year deal for memory supply and joint R&D on next-generation memory and AI architecture. Related:[Nvidia taps SKT for physical AI](/ai-machine-learning/nvidia-taps-skt-for-physical-ai) ##### AI cloud Nvidia said the partnership would enable memory supply "to keep pace with Nvidia's infrastructure roadmap and the sustained buildout of AI infrastructure worldwide." "Together, we are codeveloping the next generation of memory for AI factories and applying AI to how we design and manufacture semiconductors," Chey said. SK Telecom's role in the enlarged partnership is to [build a gigawatt AI cloud](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/sk-telecom-ai-infrastructure), expected to come online in 2027, Nvidia said.  The GPU-based cloud is aimed at serving rising demand for AI infrastructure and will be built on Nvidia's[DSX platform](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/products/dsx/), designed to produce low-cost tokens at high energy efficiency. It will support training, inference and agentic workloads, including sovereign AI, initially for South Korea but with plans to extend to elsewhere in Asia.  Nvidia also announced AI infrastructure deals with Naver and the LG Group. Naver, whose services range from search to shopping and cloud,[will use Nvidia chips to grow its sovereign AI infrastructure](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/naver-ai-infrastructure), starting at 55MW but with the aim of expanding to gigawatt scale. It will also use Nvidia chips to accelerate its data center business and to boost its sovereign AI capacity in Europe and the Middle East. Nvidia's[partnership with LG](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-and-lg-group-ai-factory/) covers multiple emerging technologies including robotics, autonomous driving, data centers and cloud. The AI factory will provide LG Group with accelerated computing infrastructure to train and validate AI-based applications across the company, Nvidia said. ## About the Author [Robert Clark](/author/robert-clark) Contributing Editor, Light Reading Robert Clark is an independent technology editor and researcher based in Hong Kong. Infra & Chips.titleZh: LG Teams with NVIDIA to Shape the Future with M.A.P. (Mobility / AI Infra / Physical AI) | - KIPOST
Infra & Chips.content: # LG Teams with NVIDIA to Shape the Future with M.A.P. (Mobility / AI Infra / Physical AI) 2026-06-08     PR Newswire SEOUL, South Korea, June 8, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- LG and NVIDIA are expanding their strategic collaboration across industries, Physical AI, AI Infra and mobility jointly drawing the future map of industry. LG and NVIDIA held a Top Management Meeting in Yeouido, Seoul, attended by Kwang Mo Koo, Chairman and CEO of LG Corp., Jensen Huang, CEO of NVIDIA, and other top executives. The meetings, attended by Kwang Mo Koo, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, and heads of major companies, expand the scope of mid- to long-term strategic cooperation leading industrial innovation in the AI era. The expanded collaboration combines NVIDIA's cutting-edge AI technologies with LG's manufacturing and infrastructure capabilities to implement AI most quickly and comprehensively in customers' daily lives and industrial sites. The collaboration between LG, which possesses decades of manufacturing innovation know-how and vast life data assets accumulated through customer touchpoints around the world, and NVIDIA, is expected to accelerate global AI innovation across both industry and daily life. Kwang Mo Koo, Chairman and CEO of LG Corp., said, "Together with NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, we had a very in-depth and inspiring discussion on strategic cooperation that will transform future industries," adding, "The blueprint for the AI ecosystem envisioned by NVIDIA aligns with LG's future direction of creating meaningful changes in customers' daily lives and global industrial sites. With today's meeting as a starting point, we will further solidify our partnership by combining the unique capabilities of both companies." "Korea is extraordinary at manufacturing, mechatronics and AI, and the fusion of these strengths will make robotics and physical AI a major growth sector for the country," said Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of NVIDIA. "With NVIDIA DSX and physical AI platforms, LG can extend its leadership from homes and vehicles to factories and AI infrastructure, creating new growth opportunities across the intelligent systems that will shape daily life and industry." **1. Cooperation in Physical AI and Robotics** The two companies will pursue a win-win strategy in the field of Physical AI, spanning manufacturing to robotics, by maximizing synergies based on each company's core capabilities. LG has accumulated production technology data and know-how from global manufacturing sites, while NVIDIA provides NVIDIA Isaac, Omniverse and Cosmos AI and simulation technologies. By combining these strengths, the two companies plan to enhance AI-driven manufacturing competitiveness, build an autonomous manufacturing ecosystem in which the entire process from raw material procurement to production, logistics, and customer delivery is connected in real time through data and AI, and establish it as a new global smart factory standard. The two companies will also pursue development of NVIDIA's next-generation robot foundation model GR00T, further strengthening cooperation in robotics. LG and NVIDIA will advance robot development capabilities and performance through strategic cooperation across the entire robotics field, from data collection and generation, simulation, training, and actions, in a wide range of robots including humanoids and logistics robots. LG Innotek will serve as the eyes and ears of robots based on its world-class optical technologies, and will develop high-performance sensing modules and optical components optimized for NVIDIA AI infrastructure. Furthermore, LG CNS is building an ecosystem that enables anyone to easily adopt AI robots in manufacturing and logistics sites. By integrating NVIDIA's robotics technologies, including NVIDIAIsaac open robotics frameworks, Cosmos open world models and Isaac GR00T open robotic foundation models, into its industrial robot platform, "PhysicalWorks," the company is accelerating the AI transformation of logi...

Infra & Chips.titleZh: Nvidia's AI PC push banks on unproven demand beyond niche users - Reuters
Infra & Chips.content: [Skip to main content](#main-content) # Nvidia's AI PC push banks on unproven demand beyond niche users By [Aditya Soni](/authors/aditya-soni/) and [Anhata Rooprai](/authors/anhata-rooprai/) * Companies * [Advanced Micro Devices Inc](/company/advanced-micro-devices-inc/) * [Apple Inc](/company/apple-inc/) * [Asustek Computer Inc](/company/asustek-computer-inc/) June 8 (Reuters) - Nvidia's [(NVDA.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/NVDA.O) entry into the AI PC market with its [RTX Spark superchip](https://www.reuters.com/world/china/nvidia-ceo-kick-off-dominate-computex-gathering-taipei-2026-05-31/) last week is less a breakthrough for regular users than a high-stakes bet that a largely unproven concept can find wider appeal, analysts said. At the Computex trade show in Taiwan, ​the chipmaker pitched a future where laptops run large AI models locally and act as personal digital agents, no cloud needed. The Reuters Inside Track newsletter is your essential guide during the World Cup. Sign up [here.](/newsletters/inside-track/?location=article-paragraph&redirectUrl=%2Fworld%2Fchina%2Fnvidias-ai-pc-push-banks-unproven-demand-beyond-niche-users-2026-06-08%2F) It's ‌a claim that PC makers HP [(HPQ.N), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/HPQ.N) and Dell [(DELL.N), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/DELL.N) have made for nearly three years now, only to be met with skepticism from Wall Street and consumers, with high prices outweighing tangible benefits. Nvidia, though, appears to be selling a different version of the AI PC than what exists today, one aimed more at developers and content creators who ​have long favored Apple's [(AAPL.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/AAPL.O) high-end MacBook Pros. Six companies - Microsoft [(MSFT.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/MSFT.O), Asus [(2357.TW), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/2357.TW), HP, Lenovo [(0992.HK), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/0992.HK), Dell, and MSI [(2377.TW), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/2377.TW) - will make PCs using the chip. ​These stocks surged after Nvidia's announcement on June 1. "RTX Spark doesn't make traditional PCs obsolete. It creates a ⁠new category between the workstation and the AI server," said Kevin Hein, analyst at Tirias Research. The chip combines a central processor, graphics engine and up ​to 128 gigabytes of unified memory, making it capable of running large AI models locally, something current AI PCs cannot do at scale. Nvidia says ​it could reshape how people interact with computers, with AI agents handling tasks such as generating videos or debugging code. Existing AI PCs, marketed heavily over the past two years, have centered on modest features like transcription or image editing and have failed to drive meaningful sales for the device manufacturers and their partners such as Arm ​and Qualcomm [(QCOM.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/QCOM.O). ## COST BARRIERS LOOM A premium price and a memory chip crunch, which has already driven up device cost, are likely to limit RTX Spark ​devices to niche adoption, analysts said. The cost "won't deter all the big computer makers from working with Nvidia on this, but the bulk of PC sales for the next ‌several years ⁠will still be more traditional Windows-based PCs with chips from Intel [(INTC.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/INTC.O), AMD and Qualcomm," said Bob O'Donnell, president at TECHnalysis Research. HP and Dell stock had been climbing even before the launch of Nvidia's superchip, up 18% and 223%, respectively, so far this year. But that rally has been driven less by AI PCs and more by a wave of corporate upgrades to Windows 11, as well as booming demand for AI infrastructure, particularly for Dell. Infra & Chips.title: LG Uplus bets big on AI data centers with $3.26B target - Light Reading
Infra & Chips.titleZh: LG Uplus bets big on AI data centers with $3.26B target - Light Reading
Infra & Chips.content: * [Data Centers](/it-infrastructure/data-centers) * [AI & Machine Learning](/digital-transformation/ai-machine-learning) * [Business Management](/business-management) * [News](/latest-news) # LG Uplus bets big on AI data centers with $3.26B target LG Uplus has placed its upcoming 200 MW Paju AI Data Center at the heart of its AIDC infrastructure strategy as it pursues inference-driven AI workloads. [Gigi Onag,](/author/gigi-onag)Senior Editor, APAC,Light Reading June 8, 2026 3 Min Read (Source: Olga Salt/Alamy Stock Photo) South Korean telecom operator LG Uplus is ramping up its AI data center (AIDC) infrastructure business with a plan to achieve 5 trillion Korean won (US$3.26 billion) in cumulative orders by 2030, growing annual sales by 15% to 20%. At the heart of the company's AIDC infrastructure strategy is the 200MW hyperscale facility now being built in Paju, Gyeonggi Province.  The facility will consist of five buildings spanning approximately 150,000 square meters (the equivalent of 21 soccer fields). It is said to be the only data center in the Seoul metropolitan area capable of supporting such a high level of power. This positions the facility as a key hub for inference-driven AI workloads. In a [statement](https://www.lguplus.com/about/en/corporation/promotion/press-kit/detail?atclNo=2000001574&pageNo=1&srchCond=titCon&srchCntn=) released last Friday, LG Uplus noted that the shift in focus of AI operations from training to "inference" has led to increased power consumption, volatility and a sharp rise in heat generation in AI data centers. Additionally, constructing data centers takes years, creating a gap between the demand and supply of AI infrastructure. Related:[AtlasEdge lands financing for European expansion](/data-centers/atlasedge-lands-financing-for-european-expansion) "The GPUs needed for AI work can be secured within a few months, but building AIDCs to accommodate them takes three to four years, which widens the gap. Additionally, AI token usage has more than doubled every quarter, highlighting delays in building AI data centers as a key risk to business progress," the company explained. ##### Accelerated AIDC construction LG Uplus plans to adopt a prefabricated modular data center construction method to accelerate time to deployment. This approach enables the offsite manufacturing of standardized main components and their onsite assembly, reducing build time by several months. It also enables the company to scale proof-of-concept projects to hyperscale facilities flexibly. The company will also rely on the "One LG" alliance to build its Paju AIDC, leveraging specialized, localized technologies across the LG Group ecosystem. LG Electronics will develop and provide advanced thermal cooling units and free-cooling chillers. LG Energy Solutions will supply high-performance uninterruptible power supply (UPS) batteries with multi-layered safety cells, which will eliminate the risk of fire and thermal runaway. LG Uplus and LS Electric will jointly develop the massive DC 800V power distribution system to handle high-density power loads. According to LG Uplus, the Paju facility is the first data center in South Korea designed to accommodate both air and liquid cooling systems simultaneously. The Direct-to-Chip technology, which was co-developed by LG Electronics, circulates cooling water via a dedicated metal plate attached directly to the GPU processors. Internal validation shows that this technology improves energy efficiency by 24% over standard air cooling. Related:[Cogent to unload 10 data centers](/data-centers/cogent-to-unload-10-data-centers) "From the initial construction, the building's load, waterproofing and piping systems were optimized for liquid cooling in order to cope with the heat generated by the GPU servers focused on inference. This allows for the efficient cooling of all types of AI chips, including GPUs, CPUs, and NPUs," the company said. Meanwhile, LG Uplus is developing an AI-based data ce...

[Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) **1. Fast action AI software:** OpenAI [launched Sites](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly9kZXZlbG9wZXJzLm9wZW5haS5jb20vY29kZXgvc2l0ZXM_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uZXdzbGV0dGVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPW5ld3NsZXR0ZXJfY3N1aXRlX2JldGEmc3RyZWFtPXRvcA/586d9e571e560373298b467cBc1d0f48f), a Codex feature that lets your staff create, host and deploy work-related web apps from prompts internally — instantly. * **Why it matters:** Custom software usually requires vendor contracts, engineering time or a long IT queue. Non-engineers can skip all that and build, ship and USE internal tools themselves. **2. AI's cheaper future:** Nvidia's RTX Spark and Microsoft's Project Solara [aim to move](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDUvMzAvbnZpZGlhLW1pY3Jvc29mdC1wY3MtYWktc3VyZmFjZS1kZWxsP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9bmV3c2xldHRlciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1uZXdzbGV0dGVyX2NzdWl0ZV9iZXRhJnN0cmVhbT10b3A/586d9e571e560373298b467cBe2976078) AI inference from the cloud onto personal devices. * **Why it matters:** Companies are starting to ration AI [as token costs rise](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDUvMTgvdG9rZW5zLWFpLXNvZnR3YXJlLWNvc3QtcHJlcC1jZW8_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uZXdzbGV0dGVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPW5ld3NsZXR0ZXJfY3N1aXRlX2JldGEmc3RyZWFtPXRvcA/586d9e571e560373298b467cBbf685d8d). Local [AI](https://www.axios.com/technology/automation-and-ai) will make usage less metered, like a shift from paying by the minute for long-distance calls to an unlimited plan. **3. Physical AI's GPT moment:** Nvidia [unveiled Cosmos 3](https://link.axios.com/click/46042235.808/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDYvMDEvbnZpZGlhLWFpLXB1c2gtY29zbW9zLTMtd29ybGQtbW9kZWw_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uZXdzbGV0dGVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPW5ld3NsZXR0ZXJfY3N1aXRlX2JldGEmc3RyZWFtPXRvcA/586d9e571e560373298b467cB18c5392a), a foundation model for physical AI. * **Why it matters:** The first wave of AI read and wrote. The next has to see, move and react to power a factory robot reaching for a part. Trained on physics rather than text, Cosmos 3 aims to be the infrastructure for that shift. 📈 *If you're a CEO or on a CEO's team: [Ask to join](https://www.axios.com/newsletters/c-suite?utm_medium=native&utm_source=axios_article&utm_campaign=csuite) Jim's new weekly Axios C-Suite newsletter.* [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) ##### What to read next
